"input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n1484\r\n\r\n## Quartermaster and live Captain controls\r\n\r\n* confirmation dialogs,\r\n* command overlays,\r\n* prepare chapter,\r\n* release chapter,\r\n* mark solved,\r\n* award artifact,\r\n* reveal silhouette,\r\n* reveal map,\r\n* reveal route,\r\n* connect artifacts,\r\n* discover/update/complete side quest,\r\n* add annotation,\r\n* add log entry,\r\n* tease finale,\r\n* update finale requirement,\r\n* pause,\r\n* resume,\r\n* undo,\r\n* failure reversal,\r\n* success reconciliation,\r\n* recent event list,\r\n* status gauges,\r\n* connection lamps.\r\n\r\n## Studio\r\n\r\n* Studio Library,\r\n* editor transitions,\r\n* dragging and sorting,\r\n* inspector,\r\n* block insertion,\r\n* block deletion,\r\n* block reordering,\r\n* validation,\r\n* autosave,\r\n* previews,\r\n* publishing,\r\n* version creation,\r\n* asset uploading,\r\n* immutable version states,\r\n* comparison flows.\r\n\r\n## Development animation showcase\r\n\r\n* every registered scene,\r\n* runtime controls,\r\n* replay,\r\n* pause,\r\n* resume,\r\n* seek,\r\n* speed,\r\n* skip,\r\n* reverse,\r\n* fallback simulation,\r\n* memory/runtime counts,\r\n* scene serial trailer.\r\n\r\nThe showcase must not be treated as proof that the real production screen is correctly integrated.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 4. Mandatory Audit Categories\r\n\r\nFor every animation or animation opportunity, classify it into all applicable categories.\r\n\r\nDo not use only three broad categories. The final matrix must be much more detailed.\r\n\r\n## Category A: Animation does not run at all\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* scene is registered but never called,\r\n* component exists but is never mounted,\r\n* Rive asset has no real `.riv` file,\r\n* animation target does not exist,\r\n* selector is wrong,\r\n* CSS state is never applied,\r\n* callback is never triggered,\r\n* route changed before animation begins,\r\n* an animation is development-only,\r\n* production renders only a fallback,\r\n* event type has no scene mapping.\r\n\r\n## Category B: Animation technically runs but is invisible\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* GSAP animates an empty target array,\r\n* the target is in a hidden source tree,\r\n* the animation runs in an unmounted section,\r\n* the target is behind another layer,\r\n* opacity or display prevents visibility,\r\n* a cloned PageFlip source receives the animation instead of the visible page,\r\n* broad selectors animate offscreen elements,\r\n* the scene completes semantically while no visible object moved.\r\n\r\n## Category C: Animation runs poorly\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* abrupt,\r\n* too fast,\r\n* too slow,\r\n* visually weak,\r\n* generic fade,\r\n* disconnected from the physical object,\r\n* poor easing,\r\n* no anticipation,\r\n* no settle,\r\n* no secondary motion,\r\n* timing does not match sound,\r\n* animation begins before the user can see it,\r\n* animation restarts unexpectedly,\r\n* animation ends with a snap,\r\n* transition does not match the visual design,\r\n* animation looks different depending on current section.\r\n\r\n## Category D: Animation only runs once\r\n\r\nDetermine whether each one-shot is:\r\n\r\n* intentionally ceremonial,\r\n* unintentionally blocked,\r\n* session-only,\r\n* tab-only,\r\n* device-only,\r\n* account-only,\r\n* campaign-only,\r\n* event-only,\r\n* replayable,\r\n* not replayable,\r\n* replayable only before refresh,\r\n* incorrectly replayed automatically.\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* `sessionStorage`,\r\n* `localStorage`,\r\n* component-local refs,\r\n* event acknowledgement,\r\n* sequence tracking,\r\n* route remount behavior,\r\n* React key behavior,\r\n* CSS animation iteration counts,\r\n* viewport `once` options,\r\n* persisted â€œseenâ€ flags.\r\n\r\n## Category E: Animation does not replay correctly\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* replay button disappears after refresh,\r\n* replay works only from the Journal,\r\n* replay mutates server state,\r\n* replay uses stale event data,\r\n* replay does not restore previous section,\r\n* replay leaves elements in a transformed state,\r\n* replay works once but fails a second time,\r\n* replay conflicts with a live event.\r\n\r\n## Category F: Wrong library owns the behavior\r\n\r\nFor every questionable animation, identify:\r\n\r\n* current owner,\r\n* correct owner,\r\n* reason,\r\n* migration recommendation,\r\n* wrappers or bridge required.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* Motion and GSAP writing transform on the same node,\r\n* GSAP page-flip scenes duplicating StPageFlip,\r\n* CSS controlling narrative order,\r\n* Lottie autoplaying independently of a GSAP ceremony,\r\n* static SVG used where a stateful Rive object is promised.\r\n\r\n## Category G: Runtime ownership conflict\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* GSAP versus Motion,\r\n* GSAP versus CSS transitions,\r\n* Motion layout versus StPageFlip,\r\n* Motion layoutId versus GSAP FLIP,\r\n* hidden PageFlip sources versus visible clones,\r\n* Rive container transforms versus external transforms,\r\n* Lottie containers moved by the wrong owner.\r\n\r\nIdentify every element with overlapping property writers.\r\n\r\n## Category H: Missing trigger\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* scene exists but has no event,\r\n* page turn occurs but no lifecycle scene is emitted,\r\n* inspection scene exists but opening the inspection never calls it,\r\n* ship-course exists but current position never triggers it,\r\n* marker-stamp exists but all map events use map-reveal,\r\n* prose-ink exists but annotations appear instantly.\r\n\r\n## Category I: Missing cleanup or lifecycle handling\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* component unmount,\r\n* route changes,\r\n* tab visibility,\r\n* element visibility,\r\n* interrupted animations,\r\n* skipped animations,\r\n* reversed animations,\r\n* failed requests,\r\n* runtime destruction,\r\n* duplicated event listeners,\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "---RESULT 3---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.3 seconds\nOutput:\n---\r\nname: control-in-app-browser\r\ndescription: \"Control the in-app Browser for opening, navigating, inspecting visible or interactive page state, clicking, typing, screenshots, and local web testing. It can have existing signed-in sessions. For semantic operations on linked resources, prefer a purpose-built connector, API, or CLI when available.\"\r\n---\r\n\r\n# Browser\r\n## Stop: choose the right surface before any browser action\r\n\r\nExplicit browser intent wins: if the user names the in-app browser or Chrome, or asks to open, show, or navigate to a page; inspect its visual or interactive state; or interact with its UI, continue with Browser and do not substitute a connector.\r\n\r\nOtherwise, treat a URL or open browser tab as context, not browser intent. Earlier Browser use does not make later semantic work browser-first. Before each semantic operation on a linked resource, you MUST query available and deferred tools for an applicable connector, API, or CLI. Reading this skill or scanning visible tools does not count. Do not initialize Browser for that operation until the query is complete. Use the non-browser tool when available. If it handles the current operation, continue the larger workflow without Browser for that operation. Use Browser when no such tool exists, the tool cannot access the resource or lacks a required capability, or UI work remains; use available browser context before asking the user to repeat it.\n\nUse this skill for browser automation tasks such as inspecting pages, navigating, testing local apps, clicking, typing, taking screenshots, and reading visible page state.\r\n\r\nIf this plugin is listed as available in the session, treat that as mandatory reading before browser work. Open and follow this skill before saying that Browser is unavailable and before falling back to standalone Playwright or Computer Use.\r\n\r\nDo not skip this skill just because Computer Use MCP tool calls are directly visible or appear easier to invoke. The presence of Computer Use tools is not evidence that Computer Use is the preferred browser surface.\n\n## Setup Documentation\nUse `await agent.documentation.get(\"<name>\")` when one of these setup topics applies:\n- `bootstrap-troubleshooting`: read when browser setup succeeds but discovery or selection fails\n- `chrome-troubleshooting`: read when Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication fails\n\n## Bootstrap\r\nThese setup details are internal. User-facing progress updates should be less technical in nature. Never mention `Node REPL`, `node_repl`, `REPL`, JavaScript sessions, module exports, reading documentation, or loading instructions unless a user is asking for that exact information. If setup or recovery is needed, describe it naturally as connecting to the browser or retrying the browser connection.\r\n\r\nThe `browser-client` module is the core entry point for browser use, and is available under `scripts/browser-client.mjs` in this plugin's root directory. ALWAYS import it using an absolute path. IMPORTANT: If this path cannot be found, stop and report that this plugin is missing `scripts/browser-client.mjs`. NEVER use the built in `browser-client` library.\r\n\r\nRun browser setup code through the Node REPL `js` tool. In this environment the callable tool id typically appears as `mcp__node_repl__js`. If it is not already available, use tool discovery for `node_repl js` without setting a result limit. You need the `js` execution tool: `js_reset` only clears state, and `js_add_node_module_dir` only changes package resolution. Do not call either helper while trying to expose `js`. If `js` is still not available, search again for `node_repl js` with `limit: 10`.\r\n\r\nInitialize the runtime once per fresh Node session. If `agent.browsers` already exists, reuse it; do not import or initialize another browser runtime.\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {\r\n  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import(\"<plugin root>/scripts/browser-client.mjs\");\r\n  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnce a browser connection is established, reuse its existing browser binding across later turns and do not reread this skill. Once you have read a browser's complete documentation, do not read it again unless you select a different browser.\r\n\r\nA tab binding is separate from its browser binding. If a later turn reports that a tab is missing, stale, closed, or not part of the current browser session, discard that tab binding and obtain or create a fresh tab from the existing browser binding. An empty `browser.tabs.list()` or `browser.user.openTabs()` result is normal after tab cleanup and does not invalidate the browser binding. Never call `agent.browsers.get*` to recover a tab; only an explicit browser-disconnected error invalidates the binding.\r\n\r\n## Browser selection\r\n\r\nThe scenarios below are for the initial browser selection only. Before calling any `agent.browsers.get*` method, reuse an existing `globalThis.browser`, `globalThis.iab`, or `globalThis.chrome` binding that already serves the task. A new user turn does not invalidate a browser binding or require another selection or documentation call.\r\n\r\nSelect the initial browser with exactly one of these scenarios, in the order\r\nshown. An explicit request for the in-app browser or Chrome always wins over URL\r\nselection. Never call `getForUrl()` when the user names a browser.\r\n\r\nApp-provided in-app-browser context is ambient UI state, not a user instruction to select or switch browsers. Only the text of the user's request can explicitly choose a browser.\r\n\r\nDo not inspect browser cookies, local storage, profiles, passwords, or session stores. Browser discovery must remain read-only.\r\n\r\nWhen authentication blocks requested browser navigation, do not replace it with web search, a search engine, another site, or another source merely to bypass sign-in.\r\n\r\n### The user explicitly requests a browser\r\n\r\nA plugin mention in the user's request explicitly names its browser.\r\n`[@Browser](plugin://browser@openai-bundled)` names the in-app browser.\r\n`[@Chrome](plugin://chrome@openai-bundled)`,\r\n`[@chrome-internal](plugin://chrome-internal@openai-bundled)`, and\r\n`[@chrome-dev](plugin://chrome-dev@openai-bundled)` name Chrome. Follow the\r\ncorresponding explicit-browser scenario below.\r\n\r\nThe in-app browser is available only when the Browser skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests the in-app browser and that skill is available, use a distinct persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.iab == null) {\r\n  globalThis.iab = await agent.browsers.get(\"iab\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await iab.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests the in-app browser but the Browser skill is not available, report that the in-app browser is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nChrome is available only when the Chrome skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests Chrome and that skill is available, use a separate persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.chrome == null) {\r\n  globalThis.chrome = await agent.browsers.get(\"extension\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await chrome.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests Chrome but the Chrome skill is not available, report that Chrome is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nAn explicit browser choice remains in force for the task. If authentication blocks the task in an explicitly selected browser, your next response must explicitly ask the user to sign in in that browser and tell you when it is ready, unless that browser's documentation provides a supported authentication flow to try first. Merely reporting that sign-in is required is not sufficient. Do not switch to another browser unless the user asks or approves the switch.\r\n\r\n### The task requires browser interaction, the user does not specify a browser, and the task has a target URL\r\n\r\nWhen the user supplies a URL or the intended URL can be reasonably inferred from the request, replace the example below with that URL and let browser-client choose the browser best suited to it:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getForUrl(\"https://example.com/\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n### The user specifies neither a browser nor a target URL\r\n\r\nUse the runtime default, which prefers the in-app browser when it is available and otherwise uses Chrome. Do not list browsers first:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getDefault();\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n## After setup\r\n\r\nIf setup succeeds but browser discovery or selection fails, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"bootstrap-troubleshooting\")` before resetting the JavaScript session or trying another browser-control mechanism.\r\n\r\nIf the failure is specific to Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"chrome-troubleshooting\")` before retrying or taking another recovery action.\r\n\r\nWhen the user did not explicitly choose a browser, a browser selected by the runtime is not a user constraint. Do not switch browsers based only on an assumption about authentication. If navigation shows that the selected browser lacks the required authentication, select another available browser before asking the user to sign in. You may select it without resetting the Node session. Preserve existing `iab`, `chrome`, and `browser` bindings when they are still useful. Existing tabs remain bound to the browser that created them. After selecting a different browser, obtain a tab from that browser before continuing and read its complete documentation.\r\n\r\nThe ability to interact directly with browsers is exposed through the `browser-client` runtime via the `agent.browsers.*` API. Before trying to interact with a selected browser for the first time, you MUST emit and read the complete documentation returned by its `documentation()` call in one go. For the initial documentation read, run the exact direct `nodeRepl.write(await <browser>.documentation());` call shown in the applicable scenario above. Do not assign the documentation to a variable, inspect its length, slice it, truncate it, summarize it, or emit only an excerpt. Do not proactively split the documentation into pages or chunks. Only if the tool output itself explicitly reports that it was truncated may you emit and read smaller chunks until you have read the documentation in its entirety.\r\n\r\nOnly the Node REPL `js` tool (`mcp__node_repl__js`) can be used to control the selected browser. Do not use external MCP browser-control tools, separate browser automation servers, or other browser skills for this surface. References to Playwright mean the in-skill `tab.playwright` API after browser-client setup.\n\n<!-- BROWSER_SKILL_EOF: This is the complete Browser skill. Do not request additional lines. -->\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "---RESULT 4---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.3 seconds\nOutput:\n18:\r\n19:- rollout_summaries/2026-07-18T01-15-26-RuIa-treasurehunt_local_windows_setup_and_validation.md (cwd=\\\\?\\C:\\Users\\kkids\\Documents\\Codex_TreasureHunt, rollout_path=\\\\?\\C:\\Users\\kkids\\.codex\\sessions\\2026\\07\\17\\rollout-2026-07-17T21-15-31-019f72ca-dfc8-7ea3-b1c5-73d7f3d7fbc2.jsonl, updated_at=2026-07-18T02:27:31+00:00, thread_id=019f72ca-dfc8-7ea3-b1c5-73d7f3d7fbc2, success; player sessions cannot call admin APIs\n- Commands: prepare/release/solve/complete chapter, prepare/release hint, reveal map, award artifact, discover/advance quest, release journal entry, pause/resume, undo, reconciliation\n- Migration: Phase 2 `20260716223000_player_companion_shell`, then Phase 3 `20260716233000_game_master_command_center`; connector-parity MySQL SQL exists and is wired through `npm run db:migrate:mysql:command-center`\n- Development data: Phase 2 multi-state presets plus Phase 3 prepared-action and audit fixtures; normal launcher restarts preserve campaign state, while `npm run db:preset -- <preset>` is the explicit reset path\n- Startup: `npm run dev:full`; validation: `npm run validate`; stop: `npm run dev:stop`\n- Known limitations: no background scheduler, multi-event rollback, automated presence cleanup, Redis fan-out, real finale/story/private media, or deployment\n- Latest full validation: 19 unit tests, 10 executed browser tests with 2 intentional shared-database skips, 16 accepted progression events, 18 audit records, preserved sequence after seed ensure, production build, and two production restart proofs\n- Source pull requests: `https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT/pull/1` and `https://github.com/Kgray44/treasurehuntSoT/pull/2`\n- Required gate: complete post-merge validation and synchronize project records before pushing `main`\n\n## Cinematic and Studio handoff\n\n- Source branch: `feature/cinematic-animation-rebuild`\n- Baseline commit: `2c07497f148707a72eac15783b88293ff0b3413d`\n- Phase completed: cinematic animation architecture and full interface rebuild\n- Version: `0.2.0`\n- Repository visibility: **public** as verified 2026-07-17; only fictional development content is permitted\n- Updated: 2026-07-17\n\n## Product surfaces\n\n- Landing: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/`\n- Player: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/tale/development-forever-treasure`\n- Game Master: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/quartermaster`\n- Development showcase: `http://127.0.0.1:3000/dev/animations`\n- Player sections: `journal`, `chart`, `treasures`, `quests`, `log`, `finale`\n\nThe landing, access gate, player journal workspace, chart, treasure altar, quest ledger, ship's log, finale chamber, and quartermaster dashboard share the physical moonlit-journal design system. The development showcase proves every authored scene, runtime control, fallback, motion mode, and the serial trailer without calling progression APIs. The showcase route returns 404 in production.\n\n## Animation architecture\n\n- `AnimationDirector` is the single transport for scene registration, playback, pause, resume, seek, speed, skip, reverse, cancellation, cleanup, and observable state.\n- GSAP owns authored timeline transforms, SVG drawing, masks, SplitText, MotionPath, labels, and callbacks.\n- Motion owns React mount/unmount, layout, navigation, dialog, and press interactions.\n- StPageFlip owns physical page turns over a shared React page model; reduced motion uses a semantic static page reader.\n- Rive is dynamically loaded from a local WebGL2 binary with CDN fallback disabled and SVG failure fallbacks.\n- Lottie is dynamically loaded from three original local JSON assets and exposes play, pause, segment, speed, direction, visibility, and destruction controls.\n- Full, gentle, and reduced policies preserve state and semantic reading order. Document and element visibility pause expensive runtimes.\n- Procedural Web Audio cues are optional, user-unlocked, disposable, and never block navigation or state transitions.\n\nSee `docs/animation/` for scene inventory, ownership rules, asset contracts, provenance, showcase operation, testing, and performance notes.\n\n## Data and security boundaries\n\n- Existing Prisma, session, allowlist projection, SSE, event ordering, and audit boundaries remain intact.\n- The showcase uses deterministic fictional props and makes no player or GM mutation requests.\n- Local animation assets are validated for expected paths, JSON shape, Rive magic bytes, and remote URL exclusion.\n- No production secrets, private story content, photographs, analytics, or new third-party network calls were added.\n\n## Development commands\n\n- Start: `npm run dev:full`\n- Stop: `npm run dev:stop`\n- Full validation: `npm run validate`\n- Animation asset validation: `npm run assets:validate`\n- Unit tests: `npm test`\n- Production build: `npm run build`\n\nOn a UNC checkout, the PowerShell scripts mirror into `%LOCALAPPDATA%/ForeverTreasureCompanion` so Node, Prisma, Next.js, and Playwright operate on a local filesystem. Local development defaults remain `development-moonwake` for the player and `kato` / `development-captain-only` for the GM; never reuse them outside the disposable local database.\n\n## Validation state\n\n- Strict TypeScript: passing\n- ESLint: passing with zero findings\n- Formatting: passing\n- Animation asset validator: 3 Lottie JSON files, 1 Rive binary, and local SVG fallbacks passing\n- Unit tests: 50 passing across 16 files\n- In-app browser: responsive landing/access/player/GM/showcase flows inspected; clean showcase load has no console warnings or errors\n- Showcase diagnostics: 60 FPS idle estimate, zero-to-one initial development long task depending on cache, expected 1 Rive / 2 Lottie / 1 PageFlip full-mode steady-state counts, no asset failures\n- Horizontal overflow: none at 390, 1440, or 2560 CSS pixels in the showcase audit\n- Full `npm run validate`: passing on 2026-07-17 with 8 applicable Playwright tests passing, 2 intentional heavy-suite project skips, 8 ordered events/audits verified, optimized production build passing, two production restarts passing, and the development showcase returning 404 in production\n- Validation artifacts: `%LOCALAPPDATA%/ForeverTreasureCompanion/validation/artifacts/validation`\n\n## Remaining production work\n\n- Replace the documented development Rive sample and all fictional copy with commissioned/private production art and story content after the repository becomes private.\n- Profile real production art on target low-end mobile hardware and repeat memory traces after sustained navigation.\n- SSE fan-out remains process-local; add Redis or equivalent before multi-instance deployment.\n- Exercise the parallel MySQL migration against MySQL 8 in CI.\n- The finale remains a safe A new subdirectory is synchronized automatically when it contains an eligible file; use an intentional `.gitkeep` only when preserving an otherwise empty directory is genuinely necessary.\n# Canonical Player Tall Tale Journal implementation record\n\nStatus: implementation and release-gate validation complete on 2026-07-18  \nRepository baseline: `main` at `0192a16`, synchronized with `origin/main` (`0` behind, `0` ahead) before edits  \nGoverning rule: **The Player Library is the place where Tall Tales are browsed. The immersive journal is the place where Tall Tales are played.**\n\n## Investigation findings\n\nThe repository contains two Player storytelling implementations:\n\n- The original campaign companion at `/tale/[campaignSlug]` already owns the reusable immersive journal work. Its implementation is centered in `src/components/player/workspace/JournalWorkspace.tsx`, `src/components/animation/PageFlipBook.tsx`, `src/animation/journal/page-model.ts`, `src/animation/journal/opening-machine.ts`, and the journal portions of `src/styles/player.css`. It includes the closed cover, attached latch, wax-seal opening, worn left/right page geometry, center binding, page stacks, StPageFlip turns, chapter tabs, reduced-motion semantic reader, keyboard page controls, and responsive single-page behavior.\n- Published Tall Tale playthroughs currently enter `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]` and render `PlayerRuntime.tsx`. That runtime receives only the current block and presents it as one centered `runtime-block` card. Completed platform playthroughs instead enter `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/archive` and render `VoyageArchive.tsx` as a separate metadata-and-list screen.\n\nThe canonical Player Library already exists at `/player/library`. Its cards are correctly grouped by invitation, waiting, active/paused, completed, replay, and closed states. The accepted-invitation waiting room at `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]` is also correctly separated from active play.\n\n## Existing data and synchronization\n\n`TallTale`, `TaleDraft`, chapters, story blocks, and immutable `PublishedTaleVersion.contentSnapshot` already form one Creator/runtime content model. `TaleSession` is the version-pinned playthrough aggregate. It stores lifecycle state, current chapter/block, ordered event sequence, variables, inventory, timestamps, and completion. `RevealState` records disclosed blocks and assets; `TaleSessionEvent` is ordered, durable, idempotent, and replayed over the existing authenticated SSE endpoint. Captain controls and helper verification both call the same progression engine.\n\nThe current Player session API safely authenticates both durable Player memberships and legacy opaque runtime cookies, but it projects only the current block. Its block shape also includes more authoring structure than an immersive Player renderer requires. The archive API correctly rebuilds released history from the session's pinned published version, but it uses a competing visual surface.\n\n## Reuse, retirement, and route changes\n\nReusable:\n\n- `PageFlipBook` and its reduced-motion fallback\n- physical cover, page, binding, stack, latch, seal, and responsive CSS\n- explicit journal-opening phase machine\n- immutable published snapshots and the existing 23-type Creator block registry\n- version-pinned `TaleSession`, reveal ledger, inventory, verification requests, durable events, SSE replay, and Captain progression controls\n- Player Library cards and invitation/waiting-room flows\n\nRetired from primary Player gameplay after verification:\n\n- the one-card-at-a-time use of `PlayerRuntime`\n- the standalone list-based use of `VoyageArchive`\n\n`PublishedBlockView` remains an isolated Studio block-preview renderer; it is not the active Player session surface.\n\nCanonical route plan:\n\n- `/player/library` remains the card library.\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]` remains the invitation/lobby/waiting route and forwards launched or completed playthroughs into the journal.\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/journal` becomes the durable-identity live and historical journal route.\n- `/play/[taleSlug]/session/[sessionId]` remains a legacy compatibility route but renders the same canonical journal component.\n- `/player/playthroughs/[playthroughId]/archive` becomes a compatibility redirect to the same historical journal instead of a second Player experience.\n\n## Implementation architecture\n\nThe implementation adds a typed journal presentation contract over the existing canonical `PublishedBlock` data rather than copying Creator content. A Player-safe journal projection exposes only revealed blocks, safe presentation metadata, current/complete progress, authorized assets, and exact edition metadata. Logic-only blocks remain durable in the event stream but do not become visible pages.\n\nOne reusable physical book frame now serves both the original campaign journal and the Tall Tale runtime. A Tall Tale page model converts released blocks into stable page IDs and explicit story, riddle, map/location, artifact, decision, objective/waiting, message, cinematic/media, and completion page modes. Active and historical sessions use the same renderer; historical mode disables mutations and continues reading from the pinned immutable version.\n\nCanonical progress remains in `TaleSession`, `RevealState`, verification records, inventory, variables, and ordered events. Per-Player reading position and journal UI preferences are persisted separately through the existing Player preference record, so browsing an older page never mutates the current block. Legacy cookie sessions use a local reading-state fallback only and do not change canonical progress.\n\nSSE remains the only live transport. Replayed event sequences refresh canonical state but do not replay already acknowledged visual triggers. A newly released current block physically turns the journal only when the Player is following live progress; otherwise the journal announces new content and offers a Return to Current Objective action.\n\nNo AR/Vision Waypoint Phase B1 documents or implementation files are part of this change. The two pre-existing untracked Phase B PDFs are protected parallel work and must not be edited, staged, or committed by this task.\n\n## Completion report\n\n### 1. Summary\n\nCards remain the browse/select surface in `/player/library`. Components created\n\n- `PhysicalJournalBook.tsx`: shared physical cover, binding, page stacks, latch, seal, tabs, and PageFlipBook frame.\n- `TallTaleJournalSession.tsx`: canonical live/resume/historical shell, opening phases, SSE reconciliation, persistence, actions, drawers, announcements, and recovery UI.\n- `TallTaleJournalPage.tsx`: parchment-native renderer for the ten Player journal modes.\n\n### 5. Components reused\n\n`PageFlipBook`, its semantic reduced-motion fallback, the existing opening-state vocabulary, the campaign journal's physical CSS/material language, the Studio block registry/published snapshot, the Tall Tale progression engine, the Player Library/lobby flow, and the authenticated SSE route remain authoritative and shared.\n\n### 6. Components retired\n\nThe unused `src/components/tales/PlayerRuntime.tsx` centered-card runtime and `src/components/platform/VoyageArchive.tsx` list archive were deleted after validation. `PublishedBlockView` remains only as a Studio preview surface.\n\n### 7. The existing session API now emits a recursively sanitized `journal` projection containing only released Player-readable content and exact edition metadata.\n\n### 9. Session event changes\n\nNo competing event channel or event taxonomy was added. Existing ordered events and authenticated SSE replay remain the transport. Progression now marks a completed block in `RevealState` and projects relevant entered/completed/hint events for page history. Client processing tracks the maximum canonical sequence and deduplicates refresh/visual effects.\n\n### 10. Content block support\n\nThe contract covers story, riddle, map, artifact, decision, objective, location verification, message, cinematic, and chapter-complete modes. All visible Creator registry blocks map into one of these modes; condition and variable blocks stay nonvisual. Presentation metadata supports spread, page-turn, paper, and ink choices while content remains canonical in Creator data.\n\n### 11. Captain synchronization\n\nCaptain and helper actions still enter `src/tall-tale/progression.ts`. Persisted state/events arrive over the existing SSE endpoint. When the Player is following live progress, a new current block turns the physical book; when reading history, the page is preserved and a live-content notice plus Return to Current Objective action appears. Reconnect starts after the last processed sequence and refreshes canonical state.\n\n### 12. Historical mode\n\nCompleted playthroughs reopen the same physical journal from the session's pinned immutable `PublishedTaleVersion`. Released pages, visited decisions, completion time, version label, and checksum remain available. Actions are disabled and the session cannot mutate.\n\n### 13. Responsive behavior\n\nDesktop emphasizes the open book with secondary contextual drawers. Medium widths compact the frame while retaining spreads. Narrow portrait and short landscape use a single readable sheet, safe-area-aware bottom tools, touch-safe actions, and a persistent current-objective return. CSS uses width/height/orientation capabilities, not user-agent detection.\n\n### 14. Accessibility behavior\n\nThe journal provides semantic headings/regions, keyboard page navigation, visible focus states, Escape-to-close drawers, labels for icon-like controls, status/live announcements, text scaling, transcripts/captions when authored, reduced-motion/direct-opening behavior, and the existing semantic non-curl reader.\n\n### 15. 1440 Ã— 900\r\n* 430 Ã— 932\r\n* 390 Ã— 844\r\n* 844 Ã— 390\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* full motion,\r\n* gentle motion,\r\n* product reduced motion,\r\n* browser reduced motion,\r\n* sound enabled,\r\n* sound disabled,\r\n* cached assets,\r\n* uncached assets,\r\n* first visit,\r\n* return visit,\r\n* refresh,\r\n* back/forward navigation,\r\n* replay,\r\n* skip,\r\n* failed requests,\r\n* slow requests,\r\n* offline/reconnect,\r\n* route unmount during animation.\r\n\r\nUse screenshots, Playwright traces, console output and performance evidence where appropriate.\r\n\r\nStore temporary evidence under an ignored audit directory such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nartifacts/animation-audit/\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not commit large generated traces or screenshots unless explicitly required by repository documentation policy.\r\n\r\n## 6.4 Visible-target verification\r\n\r\nFor each cinematic scene, record at runtime:\r\n\r\n* required target count,\r\n* optional target count,\r\n* visible target count,\r\n* duplicate target count,\r\n* target bounding boxes,\r\n* target opacity,\r\n* target display/visibility state,\r\n* current Player section,\r\n* scene instance ID,\r\n* animation owner,\r\n* actual properties animated.\r\n\r\nA scene must not be considered visually successful merely because its timeline completed.\r\n\r\n## 6.5 Replay testing\r\n\r\nFor every replayable scene:\r\n\r\n1. Play once.\r\n2. Replay immediately.\r\n3. Replay again.\r\n4. Navigate away and replay.\r\n5. Refresh and replay.\r\n6. Switch motion mode and replay.\r\n7. Skip midway and replay.\r\n8. Interrupt with another scene.\r\n9. Verify no server mutation occurred.\r\n10. Verify cleanup returned all runtime counts to expected values.\r\n\r\n## 6.6 One-shot analysis\r\n\r\nFor every animation that does not automatically repeat, determine exactly why.\r\n\r\nDo not write vague statements such as â€œruns once.â€\r\n\r\nWrite:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nRuns once per:\r\n- component mount\r\n- browser tab\r\n- sessionStorage key\r\n- device\r\n- campaign\r\n- acknowledged event\r\n- route visit\r\n- application session\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso document whether that policy is correct.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Required Per-Animation Matrix\r\n\r\nCreate a master matrix with one row per animation, effect, transition or missing opportunity.\r\n\r\nThe matrix must include at least these fields:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nID\r\nScreen\r\nRole\r\nComponent\r\nAnimation or proposed animation\r\nCurrent library\r\nCorrect library\r\nCurrent trigger\r\nExpected trigger\r\nCurrent replay policy\r\nRecommended replay policy\r\nRuns at all?\r\nVisible when run?\r\nRuns only once?\r\nWorks after refresh?\r\nWorks from every relevant section?\r\nFull mode result\r\nGentle mode result\r\nReduced mode result\r\nMobile result\r\nDesktop result\r\nRequired targets\r\nMissing targets\r\nDuplicate targets\r\nOwnership conflicts\r\nCleanup status\r\nFallback status\r\nPerformance concerns\r\nAccessibility concerns\r\nCurrent quality\r\nFailure severity\r\nRecommended change\r\nImplementation complexity\r\nImplementation priority\r\nTest coverage\r\nEvidence\r\nSource files\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe final matrix should be large. Do not impose an artificial item limit.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Required Per-Library Audit\r\n\r\n## 8.1 GSAP audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every scene,\r\n* every call site,\r\n* every selector,\r\n* every required target,\r\n* every optional target,\r\n* every empty-target possibility,\r\n* every global-root problem,\r\n* every cleanup/reversion issue,\r\n* every skip issue,\r\n* every failure branch,\r\n* every success branch,\r\n* every idle loop,\r\n* every reversible scene,\r\n* every timeline that can visually snap back,\r\n* every scene that should be split or retired.\r\n\r\nRecommend:\r\n\r\n* dedicated scene hosts,\r\n* scene instance IDs,\r\n* required-target contracts,\r\n* playback policies,\r\n* final-state policies,\r\n* interruption priorities,\r\n* semantic audio labels,\r\n* runtime visual assertions.\r\n\r\n## 8.2 Motion audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* all presence transitions,\r\n* layout transitions,\r\n* shared-layout transitions,\r\n* buttons and press effects,\r\n* dialogs,\r\n* cards,\r\n* tabs,\r\n* filters,\r\n* list updates,\r\n* section navigation,\r\n* all missing Motion opportunities in the modern platform.\r\n\r\nFind every node where Motion and GSAP may both write:\r\n\r\n* transform,\r\n* opacity,\r\n* layout,\r\n* scale,\r\n* x/y,\r\n* clip path.\r\n\r\nRecommend wrapper separation.\r\n\r\n## 8.3 StPageFlip audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* initialization,\r\n* mode changes,\r\n* page updates,\r\n* orientation,\r\n* keyboard handling,\r\n* pending turn queue,\r\n* source cloning,\r\n* visible DOM,\r\n* hidden source DOM,\r\n* failure fallback,\r\n* reduced mode,\r\n* cleanup,\r\n* page preservation,\r\n* filter-induced recreation,\r\n* responsive behavior.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether manual/programmatic GSAP page-flip scenes should be removed.\r\n\r\n## 8.4 Rive audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every contract,\r\n* every real `.riv` asset,\r\n* every fallback,\r\n* every state machine,\r\n* every input,\r\n* every screen,\r\n* production versus development behavior.\r\n\r\nCreate a detailed specification for missing project-authored Rive assets:\r\n\r\n* Invitation Seal\r\n* Journal Clasp\r\n* Voyage Compass\r\n* Finale Mechanism\r\n\r\nInclude required states, inputs, transitions and reduced-motion poses.\r\n\r\n## 8.5 Lottie audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every JSON asset,\r\n* every mount,\r\n* autoplay,\r\n* loop,\r\n* speed,\r\n* visibility pause,\r\n* document pause,\r\n* mode changes,\r\n* failure handling,\r\n* stalled-load handling,\r\n* timing relative to GSAP scenes.\r\n\r\nIdentify non-looping Lottie effects that begin before their intended semantic scene label.\r\n\r\n## 8.6 CSS audit\r\n\r\nInventory:\r\n\r\n* all keyframes,\r\n* all long transitions,\r\n* data-attribute state machines,\r\n* journal opening mechanics,\r\n* waiting-room breathing,\r\n* ambient effects,\r\n* focus/hover transitions,\r\n* reduced-motion overrides.\r\n\r\nSeparate:\r\n\r\n* material rendering,\r\n* ambient animation,\r\n* narrative orchestration.\r\n\r\nRecommend moving narrative ordering out of CSS without removing CSS material behavior.\r\n\r\n## 8.7 Web Animations API audit\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* use of `getAnimations`,\r\n* handling of `animation.finished`,\r\n* abort,\r\n* timeout,\r\n* infinite animations,\r\n* missing animation detection,\r\n* expected actor validation.\r\n\r\n## 8.8 Audio audit\r\n\r\nMap every sound cue to:\r\n\r\n* current trigger,\r\n* desired semantic label,\r\n* timing offset,\r\n* missing visual synchronization,\r\n* reduced/silent behavior.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Required Missing-Animation Blueprint\r\n\r\nThe final report must include a massive list of animations that should be added.\r\n\r\nDo not merely list names. For each proposed addition include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nScreen\r\nMoment\r\nUser benefit\r\nVisual concept\r\nCorrect library\r\nSupporting library if any\r\nTrigger\r\nDuration range\r\nReplay policy\r\nReduced-motion behavior\r\nSound cue\r\nFailure fallback\r\nPriority\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude additions for at least:\r\n\r\n* Landing and role selection\r\n* Player sign-in\r\n* Captain sign-in\r\n* Creator sign-in\r\n* Invitation ceremony\r\n* Player Library\r\n* Captain Library\r\n* New Voyage wizard\r\n* Waiting room\r\n* Journal opening\r\n* Journal pages\r\n* Chapter release\r\n* Chart\r\n* Treasure Altar\r\n* Artifact inspection\r\n* Side quests\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log\r\n* Finale\r\n* Quartermaster commands\r\n* Studio authoring\r\n* loading/error/success states\r\n* shell navigation\r\n* route transitions\r\n* theme/color-scheme transitions\r\n* offline/reconnect\r\n* notification and unseen-content states\r\n\r\nDo not reduce the list to ten or twenty items. The goal is a comprehensive experience blueprint.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Required Deliverables\r\n\r\nCreate the following files inside `Development_Docs`:\r\n\r\n## 10.1 Master audit\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Full_Audit.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThis must contain:\r\n\r\n1. Executive summary\r\n2. Audit baseline and methodology\r\n3. Current architecture\r\n4. Current library inventory\r\n5. Scene reachability report\r\n6. Animations that do not run\r\n7. Animations that run invisibly\r\n8. Animations that run poorly\r\n9. One-shot and replay analysis\r\n10. Ownership conflicts\r\n11. Wrong-library findings\r\n12. Lifecycle and cleanup findings\r\n13. Reduced-motion findings\r\n14. Performance findings\r\n15. Per-screen audit\r\n16. Per-library audit\r\n17. Massive missing-animation list\r\n18. Recommended architecture\r\n19. Severity-ranked findings\r\n20. Evidence references\r\n21. Limitations and items requiring further runtime confirmation\r\n\r\n## 10.2 Detailed animation matrix\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Audit_Matrix.csv\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe CSV must be genuinely useful for implementation planning and must contain the required fields specified earlier.\r\n\r\nDo not create a CSV containing vague one-line summaries.\r\n\r\n## 10.3 Implementation roadmap\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Implementation_Roadmap.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nOrganize implementation into no more than six major phases.\r\n\r\nA recommended phase structure is:\r\n\r\n1. **Animation truth and broken-trigger repair**\r\n2. **Ownership and scene-scoping architecture**\r\n3. **Player cinematic integration**\r\n4. **Platform, Library and Invitation motion**\r\n5. **Rive/Lottie/PageFlip completion**\r\n6. **Performance, accessibility and final polish**\r\n\r\nFor each phase include:\r\n\r\n* exact scope,\r\n* dependencies,\r\n* files likely affected,\r\n* libraries involved,\r\n* acceptance criteria,\r\n* tests,\r\n* risks,\r\n* work that can happen in parallel,\r\n* work that must not happen in parallel.\r\n\r\n## 10.4 Animation test plan\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Test_Plan.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude:\r\n\r\n* unit tests,\r\n* component tests,\r\n* end-to-end tests,\r\n* visual checkpoint tests,\r\n* target-integrity tests,\r\n* replay tests,\r\n* one-shot tests,\r\n* every-event/every-section matrix,\r\n* mode matrix,\r\n* viewport matrix,\r\n* interruption tests,\r\n* performance tests,\r\n* memory-leak tests,\r\n* fallback tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Severity and Priority Rules\r\n\r\nUse these severity levels:\r\n\r\n## P0 - Broken core experience\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* a major ceremony is invisible,\r\n* animation blocks interaction indefinitely,\r\n* route cannot complete,\r\n* reduced-motion accessibility failure,\r\n* production asset silently does not exist,\r\n* server event is acknowledged despite no visible required presentation when presentation is mandatory.\r\n\r\n## P1 - Major quality or consistency failure\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* wrong object animates,\r\n* duplicate objects animate,\r\n* replay is unavailable,\r\n* animation differs unpredictably by section,\r\n* severe library ownership collision,\r\n* visible snapback,\r\n* page-turn setting does not apply.\r\n\r\n## P2 - Noticeable polish deficiency\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* weak easing,\r\n* generic fade,\r\n* missing secondary motion,\r\n* missing form transition,\r\n* poor loading state,\r\n* abrupt filtering.\r\n\r\n## P3 - Enhancement\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* richer ambient motion,\r\n* optional parallax,\r\n* subtle hover refinement,\r\n* additional material response.\r\n\r\nPrioritize correctness and consistency before adding decorative layers.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Required Final Recommendations\r\n\r\nThe final audit must answer all of the following:\r\n\r\n1. Which animations are completely broken?\r\n2. Which animations do not run in production?\r\n3. Which animations run only in the development showcase?\r\n4. Which animations run but target invisible elements?\r\n5. Which animations depend on the Playerâ€™s current section?\r\n6. Which animations run once?\r\n7. Which one-shot policies are correct?\r\n8. Which one-shot policies should change?\r\n9. Which animations cannot replay after refresh?\r\n10. Which scenes have no real trigger?\r\n11. Which scenes should be retired?\r\n12. Which scenes should be connected?\r\n13. Which behaviors use the wrong library?\r\n14. Which nodes have conflicting owners?\r\n15. Which Rive objects are currently static fallbacks?\r\n16. Which Lottie effects restart incorrectly?\r\n17. Which PageFlip behaviors fail after mode changes?\r\n18. Which CSS phase waits can hang or skip?\r\n19. Which animations are missing from modern platform screens?\r\n20. What should be added to make the entire experience feel unified and premium?\r\n21. What exact implementation sequence should follow?\r\n22. Which work can be done in parallel?\r\n23. What automated tests are required before declaring the animation system complete?\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Hard Boundaries\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* broadly implement fixes during this audit,\r\n* rewrite the entire animation architecture,\r\n* replace all libraries with GSAP,\r\n* replace all libraries with Motion,\r\n* delete Rive because final assets are missing,\r\n* replace StPageFlip with a homegrown book animation,\r\n* change unrelated product functionality,\r\n* alter narrative or personal content,\r\n* expose private content,\r\n* interfere with concurrent animation-fix work,\r\n* stage the entire repository,\r\n* claim runtime confirmation without actually running the relevant flow,\r\n* claim an animation works merely because a timeline completed,\r\n* claim a fallback is a real Rive animation,\r\n* produce only a short summary.\r\n\r\nTemporary non-invasive instrumentation is acceptable if required for investigation, but it must either:\r\n\r\n* remain isolated and clearly documented, or\r\n* be reverted before task completion.\r\n\r\nThe final committed application source should remain unchanged unless there is an extremely small audit-support correction that is separately justified. Documentation files are the intended output.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Quality Bar\r\n\r\nThe audit is complete only when:\r\n\r\n* every registered scene has a reachability status,\r\n* every real animation call site has been inventoried,\r\n* every production animation library has a status,\r\n* all known hypotheses have been verified or disproved,\r\n* one-shot and replay behavior is documented per animation,\r\n* broken and invisible animation paths are identified,\r\n* ownership conflicts are mapped,\r\n* the modern platform and legacy cinematic paths are compared,\r\n* every major screen has a missing-animation blueprint,\r\n* the audit matrix is extensive,\r\n* the implementation roadmap is actionable,\r\n* and the test plan would prevent the same regressions from returning.\r\n\r\nThe result should be detailed enough that future Codex implementation prompts can use the audit as the governing source of truth and implement one phase at a time without guessing.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Final Response Format\r\n\r\nAt completion, provide:\r\n\r\n1. Exact baseline audited.\r\n2. Git/worktree safety status.\r\n3. Count of animation items inventoried.\r\n4. Count by category:\r\n\r\n   * broken,\r\n   * invisible,\r\n   * poor quality,\r\n   * one-shot,\r\n   * replay-defective,\r\n   * wrong library,\r\n   * ownership conflict,\r\n   * missing animation,\r\n   * needs runtime verification.\r\n5. Top P0 findings.\r\n6. Top P1 findings.\r\n7. Deliverable file paths.\r\n8. Tests and runtime flows executed.\r\n9. Anything that could not be verified.\r\n10. Confirmation that concurrent source work was preserved.\r\n11. 0\nWall time: 0.7 seconds\nOutput:\n===== docs/animation/architecture.md =====\r\n# Animation architecture\n\nThe cinematic layer is an orchestration system, not a set of component timers. `AnimationProvider` owns one `AnimationDirector`; every meaningful scene is registered by name and built as a GSAP timeline inside a scoped `gsap.context`. Motion handles component presence and direct interaction, StPageFlip handles the journal's physical page surface, Rive owns stateful vector objects, and Lottie owns ambient or illustrative loops.\n\n## Server-synchronized sequence\n\nFor an authenticated mutation, the director starts the real request immediately and plays only the non-authoritative opening while the request is pending. A slow request enters a bounded idle loop. Success content cannot start until the server accepts the mutation. A rejection runs the scene's failure branch and leaves the existing public snapshot in place.\n\n```mermaid\nsequenceDiagram\n  participant UI\n  participant Director\n  participant API\n  UI->>Director: play(scene, operation)\n  Director->>API: start operation immediately\n  Director->>Director: opening timeline\n  alt operation is still pending\n    Director->>Director: await-server idle loop\n  end\n  API-->>Director: success or failure\n  alt success\n    Director->>Director: success timeline\n    Director-->>UI: reconcile public snapshot\n  else failure\n    Director->>Director: failure/reversal timeline\n    Director-->>UI: preserve state and report error\n  end\n```\n\nThe player consumes one SSE connection. Events are sorted through one promise queue, mapped to a registered scene, presented, acknowledged, and then reconciled from a fresh server-filtered snapshot. Navigation, page turns, and decorative loops do not create additional event streams.\n\n## Lifecycle and control contract\n\nThe director exposes play, queue, pause, resume, seek by progress or label, speed from 0.25x to 2x, skip, supported reverse, cancel, and kill. It cancels outstanding waits, kills current timelines, reverses cleanup registration order, reverts SplitText/GSAP contexts, and releases property ownership on completion or unmount. Document visibility pauses an active timeline; a user-paused timeline does not resume merely because the tab becomes visible.\n\nEvery scene reports a live snapshot: scene, phase, label, progress, speed, mode, queue depth, pause state, and error. The development showcase and compact runtime controls consume this same state instead of maintaining a second playback model.\n\n## Motion modes\n\n`full`, `gentle`, and `reduced` are product modes. The browser's `prefers-reduced-motion` always resolves to `reduced`, even when the saved product preference says otherwise.\n\n- Full preserves authored distance, duration, ambience, particles, page curl, and camera-like framing.\n- Gentle uses 58% duration, 42% distance, fewer decorative effects, and shorter page turns while preserving narrative order.\n- Reduced uses immediate state changes, no spatial travel or ambient loops, a representative Lottie frame, stable Rive poses, and an accessible paged journal in place of page curl.\n\nModes do not change server state, content order, or acknowledgement rules. Skip also preserves those invariants: it renders the final visual state and then reconciles truth.\n\n## Adding a journal page type\n\nAdd the discriminated `kind` and safe fields to `JournalPage` in `src/animation/journal/page-model.ts`, generate a stable ID from public snapshot keys rather than array position, render that kind once in `JournalWorkspace`, and include it in the semantic/reduced page path. Never pass raw database records into the model. Extend the page-model tests for locked-content filtering, stable indices after a snapshot refresh, even spread padding, and chapter navigation; StPageFlip should receive the resulting React HTML through `updateFromHtml`, not a replacement instance.\n\n## Failure boundaries\n\nRive, Lottie, and StPageFlip are dynamically imported. Each has a local SVG or React fallback. Failed visual assets are recorded for the development metrics panel but do not block authentication, progression, reading, navigation, or GM commands. No animation failure is allowed to roll back a committed server transaction.\n===== docs/animation/assets.md =====\r\n# Animation assets\n\nAll production assets are local, registered, and paired with a fallback. Runtime code never loads artwork from a CDN.\n\n| Asset family                     | Location                          | Loading                                             | Failure behavior                              |\n| -------------------------------- | --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |\n| Original Lottie shape animations | `public/animations/lottie`        | Critical waves at entry; fog at idle; ink on intent | Static local representative SVG               |\n| Rive binaries                    | `public/animations/rive`          | Intent/development only in the current phase        | Project-authored SVG/CSS stateful fallback    |\n| Static runtime fallbacks         | `public/animations/stills`        | Immediate                                           | Accessible label remains if image cannot draw |\n| Original illustrations           | `public/illustrations`            | Browser-native local SVG                            | Text/list views remain authoritative          |\n| Material texture                 | `public/textures/paper-grain.svg` | CSS background                                      | Solid parchment token                         |\n\n`npm run assets:validate` parses each Lottie JSON contract, checks local paths and fallbacks, verifies the local Rive binary signature and size, and ensures no registered animation URL is remote. The validator runs in the complete release gate.\n\nAsset contracts record key, local path, intended pages, renderer or state machine, motion-mode behavior, loading priority, dimensions, provenance, and fallback. Add the contract and license record before adding an asset to a component.\n\nPerformance policy: use SVG Lottie for the current small shape animations; do not autoplay hidden or reduced-motion media; pause when the document is hidden; load noncritical effects at idle or first intent; destroy runtime instances on unmount; and avoid shipping source-editor files to the browser.\n===== docs/animation/library-ownership.md =====\r\n# Animation library ownership\n\nEach visual property has one owner at a time. The development registry warns when two runtimes claim a conflicting property on the same element.\n\n| Runtime    | Owns                                                                                                                                                              | Must not own                                                                                      |\n| ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |\n| GSAP       | Cinematic sequencing, SVG draw/morph/motion paths, SplitText reveals, coordinated transforms, filters, opacity, and clip paths on `[data-gsap-owned]` scene parts | Button hover/tap feedback, React presence, journal page physics, Rive internals, Lottie internals |\n| Motion     | React enter/exit, layout/presence transitions, press/hover/focus feedback, small draggable or spring interactions                                                 | Long narrative timelines or any GSAP-owned property during a scene                                |\n| StPageFlip | Page curl, book orientation, manual/programmatic turns, page index                                                                                                | Content reveals inside a page or section navigation                                               |\n| Rive       | State machine and inputs inside one stateful vector object                                                                                                        | Page-level orchestration or server timing                                                         |\n| Lottie     | Playback inside its own container                                                                                                                                 | DOM outside that container or authoritative state                                                 |\n| CSS        | Static layout, material appearance, restrained ambient keyframes                                                                                                  | Narrative order or properties claimed by an active runtime                                        |\n\n`claimAnimationOwnership` records owner/property claims in a `WeakMap`, adds a diagnostic `data-animation-owner`, and releases the claim during director cleanup. GSAP scene roots use `gsap.context(..., root)` so selector scope and reversion stay local.\n\nNested ownership is encouraged: Motion may animate a card's outer wrapper while GSAP animates a dedicated inner scene part, or GSAP may move a Lottie container while Lottie draws only within it. Two runtimes must not write `transform` or `opacity` to the same element.\n\nThe rule for additions is simple: choose the smallest runtime that naturally owns the behavior, place its target on a dedicated wrapper when necessary, declare the ownership in markup, and add cleanup before the scene begins.\n===== docs/animation/lottie-assets.md =====\r\n# Lottie assets\n\nThe three Lottie files are original Forever Treasure shape-layer animations with local SVG fallbacks.\n\n| Key             | File                 | Composition | Use                                 | Full / gentle / reduced                                    |\n| --------------- | -------------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |\n| `moonlit-waves` | `moonlit-waves.json` | 1200 × 320  | Landing and access ambience         | Loop / 0.65x / representative frame                        |\n| `rolling-fog`   | `rolling-fog.json`   | 1200 × 420  | Harbor, chart, and finale depth     | Slow loop / lower speed and opacity / representative frame |\n| `ink-bloom`     | `ink-bloom.json`     | 320 × 320   | Journal, log, and quest punctuation | One shot / short one shot / static ink mark                |\n\n`LottieEffect` imports `lottie-web` only on the client, loads JSON with `fetch` from the same origin, mounts to its own container, exposes play/pause/stop/speed controls where requested, pauses with page visibility, and destroys the animation on unmount. A fetch, parse, import, or render failure swaps to the registered local fallback without hiding content.\n\nWhen adding a file, remove unused metadata, keep paths and image references local, prefer shape layers over embedded raster images, add a representative still, register its pages/motion behavior/provenance, and run `npm run assets:validate` plus the showcase error-path check.\n===== docs/animation/performance.md =====\r\n# Animation performance\n\nThe animation budget prioritizes input response and readable state over decoration.\n\n- Keep the main thread responsive at 60 Hz where possible; no single authored animation callback should perform layout-heavy work per frame.\n- Development metrics flag `PerformanceObserver` long tasks and show rolling FPS, active scene, mounted GSAP/PageFlip/Rive/Lottie counts, visibility, and asset failures.\n- GSAP targets are cached by scene construction, SVG paths are local, and SplitText is reverted after each scene.\n- Motion animates `transform` and `opacity` on dedicated wrappers. Layout animation is reserved for small component transitions.\n- Lottie and Rive are dynamic client imports. Rive uses the offscreen renderer, disables unneeded listeners, and forbids runtime CDN fallback. Noncritical assets mount at idle/intent.\n- Hidden-document playback pauses. Reduced motion disables ambient loops and page curl. Unmounted runtimes are destroyed and timeline contexts are reverted.\n- StPageFlip clones only the current React page model, uses one instance per visible book, updates in place, and destroys the instance before its host is cleared.\n- Decorative textures and particles have ceilings in `motionPolicy`; the product can switch to gentle or reduced mode without changing content.\n\nThe showcase trailer runs scenes serially and is intended as a stress audit. Watch for counts that do not return to zero, rising asset failures, repeated long tasks, and FPS that stays below 45 on the target hardware. Investigate a regression before adding more visual layers.\n\n## Latest development observation\n\nThe 2026-07-17 in-app browser review held at a 60 FPS estimate while idle and during the sampled trailer start/skip flow. The initial development compilation and asset load registered between zero and one long task depending on cache state; the counter did not continue rising while idle. The full-mode steady state reported one Rive instance, two Lottie instances, one StPageFlip instance, zero GSAP timelines while idle, and no asset failures. Manual destroy/reset returned the Lottie count from two to one and then restored it to two without a console error. At 390, 1440, and 2560 CSS pixels, document scroll width equaled client width.\n\nThese are development diagnostics on one workstation, not a portable performance benchmark. The production showcase is intentionally unavailable, so release profiling should exercise the real landing, access, player, and quartermaster surfaces under representative device throttling.\n\nProduction review should include browser performance traces on the actual low-end mobile target, real final Rive binaries, throttled 4G/local-cache behavior, and memory snapshots after repeated section navigation. The current development metrics are diagnostics, not production telemetry.\n===== docs/animation/rive-assets.md =====\r\n# Rive assets\n\n## Production contracts\n\nInvitation seal, journal clasp, voyage compass, and finale mechanism have explicit state-machine contracts but deliberately use local original SVG/CSS fallbacks in production. No claim is made that these fallbacks are `.riv` files. When final project-authored binaries arrive, set each contract's local `path`, confirm artboard/state-machine/input names in the showcase, update provenance, and retain the fallback.\n\n| Object           | State machine      | Required states                                                        | Current production asset     |\n| ---------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------- |\n| Invitation seal  | `Invitation Seal`  | idle, hover, pressed, listening, opening, rejected                     | `seal-fallback.svg`          |\n| Journal clasp    | `Journal Clasp`    | locked, awake, opening, open                                           | `journal-clasp-fallback.svg` |\n| Voyage compass   | `Voyage Compass`   | idle, bearing, arrived                                                 | `compass-fallback.svg`       |\n| Finale mechanism | `Finale Mechanism` | dormant, teased, sealed, partial, ready, unlocking, unlocked, complete | `finale-fallback.svg`        |\n\n## Development runtime proof\n\n`rating-animation.riv` is a development-only runtime sample from the official `rive-wasm` repository. It proves local WebGL2 loading, state-machine discovery, input reporting, lifecycle cleanup, and fallback handling. It is omitted by the production contract gate and is never presented as Forever Treasure artwork.\n\n- Source: `https://github.com/rive-app/rive-wasm/tree/master/js/examples/rating-animation`\n- License: MIT, Copyright 2020-2021 Rive; retained beside the binary as `LICENSE-RIVE-MIT.txt`\n- Size: 16,229 bytes\n- SHA-256: `CB67C07896FA35DA41A976B61499277EF2B009D6D8858C11BD671C0DF5BC9630`\n- State machine: `State Machine 1`\n- Loading: dynamic client import, `useOffscreenRenderer: true`, `shouldDisableRiveListeners: true`, CDN fallback disabled\n\nThe runtime reports available inputs to the showcase. Boolean, number, and trigger controls are created from that live list; unknown inputs are never guessed. Reduced motion disables autoplay and uses a stable pose or fallback.\n===== docs/animation/scenes.md =====\r\n# Scene catalog\n\nAll scene names are typed by `AnimationSceneName` and registered exactly once in `scene-registry.ts`. Server-backed scenes may define opening, waiting idle, success, and failure branches. Local presentation scenes omit the server operation but use the same controls and cleanup.\n\n| Scene                    | Purpose                                                | Principal labels                                                                                 | Reversible |\n| ------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------- |\n| `first-arrival`          | Moonlit harbor prologue and invitation reveal          | dark-sea, moonrise, title, invitation, content-readable                                          | No         |\n| `session-reentry`        | Short return to the invitation                         | horizon, invitation, content-readable                                                            | No         |\n| `player-access`          | Listen at seal while access is checked; open or reject | listening, await-server, seal-released/lock-rejected                                             | No         |\n| `quartermaster-login`    | Key, bolt, and cabin-door sign-in                      | key-turning, await-server, content-readable/lock-rejected                                        | No         |\n| `journal-open`           | Clasp and cover opening                                | clasp-awakens, physical-open, content-readable                                                   | No         |\n| `manual-page-flip`       | Showcase/manual page turn wrapper                      | physical-open                                                                                    | No         |\n| `programmatic-page-flip` | Showcase/programmatic page turn wrapper                | physical-open                                                                                    | No         |\n| `chapter-heading`        | Focus a released chapter heading                       | attention, ink-heading                                                                           | No         |\n| `prose-ink`              | Reveal story text by masked lines                      | attention, ink-story                                                                             | No         |\n| `seal-break`             | Focused seal-to-parchment transition                   | attention, seal, parchment                                                                       | No         |\n| `chapter-release`        | Complete omen-to-active chapter ceremony               | omen, attention, seal, parchment, ink-heading, ink-story, ink-objective, ink-riddle, map, active | No         |\n| `map-reveal`             | Part fog, stamp location, reveal route                 | fog-gathering, fog-parting, marker-stamp                                                         | Yes        |\n| `route-draw`             | Draw a released chart path                             | route-drawing                                                                                    | Yes        |\n| `marker-stamp`           | Stamp a new chart marker                               | marker-stamp                                                                                     | No         |\n| `ship-course`            | Move ship token along an authored path                 | ship-underway                                                                                    | Yes        |\n| `artifact-award`         | Silhouette, light sweep, and relic placement           | velvet-darkening, silhouette, light-sweep, artifact-settled                                      | No         |\n| `artifact-inspection`    | Reveal engraving/detail in the dialog                  | engraving-reveal                                                                                 | No         |\n| `artifact-connection`    | Draw a relationship between relics                     | connection-drawing                                                                               | Yes        |\n| `quest-discovery`        | Unfold note and connect red thread                     | note-unfolds                                                                                     | No         |\n| `quest-complete`         | Apply physical completion stamp                        | completion-stamp                                                                                 | No         |\n| `log-entry`              | Write date/text and stamp symbol                       | date-written                                                                                     | No         |\n| `finale-tease`           | Wake rings without unlocking content                   | fog-gathering, mechanism-wakes, core-sealed                                                      | No         |\n| `finale-requirement`     | Illuminate one safe symbolic requirement               | requirement-activates                                                                            | Yes        |\n| `prepare-chapter`        | GM ready-state instrument response                     | command-armed, await-server, command-confirmed                                                   | No         |\n| `mark-solved`            | GM solved-state confirmation                           | command-armed, await-server, command-confirmed                                                   | No         |\n| `pause`                  | Bring campaign instruments to rest                     | command-armed, await-server, campaign-paused                                                     | No         |\n| `resume`                 | Restore campaign instruments                           | command-armed, await-server, campaign-underway                                                   | No         |\n| `undo`                   | Rewind the most recent durable projection              | command-armed, await-server, state-restored                                                      | No         |\n\nNew scenes belong in `src/animation/scenes`, should use the shared duration/distance helpers, must expose semantic labels for debugging and accessibility testing, and must be registered and added to the showcase before use in product UI.\n===== docs/animation/showcase.md =====\r\n# Animation showcase\n\n`/dev/animations` is a development-only route. The server calls `notFound()` when `NODE_ENV` is production, so no lab UI or sample asset is exposed by a production build.\n\nThe showcase provides:\n\n- Library filters for GSAP, Motion, StPageFlip, Rive, and Lottie.\n- All 28 registered product scenes plus eight focused library/asset demonstrations.\n- Play, pause, resume, restart, skip, supported reverse, progress scrubber, 0.25–2x speed, fullscreen, reset, and motion-mode controls.\n- Keyboard controls: Space play/pause, R restart, S skip, Left/Right seek, Escape exit fullscreen.\n- A serial trailer that previews the system without calling progression, access, or GM APIs.\n- StPageFlip manual/programmatic controls; Rive live input controls/status; Lottie play/pause/segment/speed/direction/destroy; and local asset/fallback/error states.\n- Live FPS, long-task, scene, visibility, mounted-runtime, and asset-failure diagnostics.\n\nThe showcase uses generic development text and local artwork. It must never make authenticated mutations or contain unreleased story content. Add every new scene and every new runtime asset here before integrating it into a production surface.\n\nThe `TEST ANIMATIONS` button is rendered only in development on player and GM surfaces and links to the lab. It is intentionally absent from production bundles at the UI boundary as well as guarded at the route.\n===== docs/animation/testing.md =====\r\n# Animation testing\n\nThe animation test strategy separates deterministic orchestration from browser rendering.\n\n## Automated layers\n\n- Unit tests cover motion-mode resolution/scaling, ownership conflicts and release, asset registration, scene completeness, operation/animation synchronization, journal page-model edge cases, and StPageFlip reduced/failure behavior.\n- The asset validator parses local Lottie files, verifies all contract/fallback paths, rejects remote animation URLs, and validates the Rive binary envelope.\n- Existing acceptance tests exercise first arrival, skip, access failure/success, page opening, live SSE chapter release, semantic ceremony stages, reduced motion, refresh persistence, GM confirmation, and accessibility.\n- The production build catches client/server boundary errors and confirms the development showcase is not routable in production.\n\nRun the whole gate with `npm run validate`. Fast focused commands are `npm test`, `npm run assets:validate`, `npm run typecheck`, and `npm run lint`.\n\n## Manual showcase pass\n\nIn development, open `/dev/animations` and verify:\n\n1. Every scene plays, pauses, resumes, restarts, skips, seeks, and honors speed.\n2. Reversible scenes reverse; unsupported scenes leave state intact.\n3. Full, gentle, browser-reduced, and product-reduced modes preserve readable content and focus order.\n4. StPageFlip manual, keyboard, and programmatic turns report the correct page/orientation and cleanly reset.\n5. Rive reports local load/input state or its honest fallback; Lottie play/pause/stop/speed and forced-error paths work.\n6. Fullscreen, narrow portrait, narrow landscape, and 2560px desktop have no clipped controls or horizontal page overflow.\n7. FPS, long tasks, mounted runtime counts, visibility, and asset failures return to idle values after reset.\n\nAnimation snapshots are evidence, not pixel-perfect contracts. Stable assertions target semantic labels (`data-cinematic-stage`), final state, accessibility, and lifecycle cleanup so minor easing or antialiasing changes do not create false failures.\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "---RESULT 3---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "---RESULT 4---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n", "type": "input_text"}]
