Product surfaces should use those semantic tokens before adding route-local values. Primary, secondary, subtle, destructive, icon, disabled, loading, and focus states share one control language; disabled controls must remain readable on both ocean and parchment surfaces.\r\n+\r\n+`ProductShell` is the shared application frame for public discovery and Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces. It owns the skip link, route-aware product mark, active navigation, keyboard-safe mobile menu, workspace language, and restrained footer. Immersive routes—including the role gateway, legacy live companion, platform journal, active Tale runtime, Quartermaster, and development showcase—deliberately remain outside that chrome so story play is not visually demoted.\r\n+\r\n+`AsyncState` supplies stable loading, recoverable error, intentional empty, and status-banner patterns. Loading reserves meaningful space and names the work in progress; error states say what failed and offer an in-place retry or a safe return; empty states explain what can happen next; successful and destructive mutations use polite status or alert regions. Route-family layouts provide meaningful document titles while the root metadata remains reusable product copy.\r\n+\r\n+System copy uses the shared product vocabulary: a Creator authors a reusable **Tall Tale**, a Captain configures a version-pinned **Voyage**, an invited **Player** waits for launch, and the **Journal** is the canonical in-session and completed-history surface. The collective word _participant_ is used when a role distinction is unnecessary; _crew_ remains optional flavor rather than permission language. Personal names and event-specific language belong to authored content and invitations, not generic product controls.\r\n+\r\n ## Cinematic companion sections\r\n \r\n The shared ocean-dark, parchment, brass, moonlight, and muted-crimson tokens remain authoritative. Journal uses ink/page hierarchy; Chart uses fog and cartographic marks; Altar uses wood/brass placement; Ledger uses secret-note rhythm; Log uses an engraved timeline; Finale uses a dormant celestial mechanism. All retain the same focus ring, corner language, shadows, texture ceiling, typography scale, and motion tokens.\r\n \r\n Tokens live in `src/styles/tokens.css`: abyss navy `#061316`, ocean green `#123b3a`, parchment `#d9c69a`, antique brass `#b89551`, crimson `#782f35`, and moonlit teal `#79d2c3`. Georgia/system serif keeps story text readable; Arial is reserved for small operational labels. Paper grain, edge wear, chart lines, brass frames, restrained glow, and vignette are original CSS/SVG materials. Focus is a 3px warm-gold outline. Layers reserve 20–35 for persistent chrome, 60 for journal opening, 70 for ceremony controls, and 90 for GM confirmations.\r\n \r\n-`globals.css` now imports logical material modules: tokens, landing, player, GM, animation runtime, and development showcase. Physical section components own their nearby scene targets. Each GSAP target uses a dedicated inner element so Motion can own the interactive outer wrapper without property conflicts.\r\n+`globals.css` imports logical material modules: tokens, shell, landing, platform, Tall Tale, Studio, player, GM, animation runtime, and development showcase. Physical section components own their nearby scene targets. Each GSAP target uses a dedicated inner element so Motion can own the interactive outer wrapper without property conflicts.\r\n \r\n Motion is semantic: full, gentle, and reduced retain the same content and order. Reduced mode removes page curl, travel, parallax, particles, and ambient loops but keeps clear state changes. Typography remains one coherent DOM copy during SplitText animation; the split wrappers are reverted after the scene.\r\ndiff --git a/docs/responsive-behavior.md b/docs/responsive-behavior.md\r\nindex 25347d6..e734156 100644\r\n--- a/docs/responsive-behavior.md\r\n+++ b/docs/responsive-behavior.md\r\n@@ -1,5 +1,11 @@\r\n # Responsive behavior\r\n \r\n+## Product shell and non-immersive workspaces\r\n+\r\n+Public discovery and Player, Captain, and Creator workspaces use a capped full-width shell. Desktop keeps the product mark and route-aware navigation in one sticky row. At compact widths, a named Menu control opens an overlaid navigation panel, moves focus to its first link, closes on Escape or backdrop activation, and returns focus to the trigger. The page remains available to assistive technology in document order, and the skip link targets the shared main-content wrapper.\r\n+\r\n+Public catalog filters collapse from four columns to one readable stack. Player library controls disappear when the library is genuinely empty. Captain section tabs use two balanced rows at phone widths rather than a horizontally scrolling strip; voyage and Studio cards expand at wide widths instead of leaving empty grid tracks. All controls retain a minimum touch height, shell padding includes safe-area insets, and mobile product chrome never overlays the landing role actions.\r\n+\r\n ## Complete shell\r\n \r\n Desktop uses the available monitor as a layered story workspace with persistent one-action navigation and compact objective access. Mobile switches to a single content column, labeled bottom navigation, safe-area padding, separate section views, and a compact return-to-clue control. The map always has a text alternative. Artifact inspection becomes a full-screen sheet on narrow screens. Fullscreen mode removes secondary chrome without removing navigation or the objective.\r\n@@ -8,4 +14,4 @@ Above 1100px, chart, open journal, and relic display form a three-region physica\r\n \r\n The published Tall Tale journal follows the same physical rules while keeping supporting controls secondary. Desktop preserves the dominant open book and contextual side drawers. Compact widths reduce header and tray density while retaining two-page structure. Phone portrait and short landscape use one readable sheet, full-width bottom drawers for chapters/maps/artifacts/messages, touch-safe actions, and a persistent Return to Current Objective control when the reader is behind live progress. Text scaling changes ink size without transforming the entire interface, and reduced motion replaces ceremonial motion with direct, semantically equivalent state changes.\r\n \r\n-Validated viewports: 1920×1080, 1440×900, 390×844, 430×932, and 844×390. 2560×1440 was layout-reviewed through the same capped workspace rules; automated retained capture remains next-pass work.\r\n+Validated viewports: 2560×1440, 1920×1080, 1440×900, 390×844, 430×932, and 844×390. Detailed animation test and manual leak/profile instructions are in `docs/animation/testing.md` and `docs/animation/performance.md`.\r\n \r\n+Product-polish unit coverage verifies public catalog search/progress filtering, no-results reset, connection-failure recovery, shell current-route semantics, skip navigation, mobile-menu focus entry and Escape restoration, and deliberate shell removal on the canonical journal. Manual browser review covers the landing explanation and mobile control placement; public search/no-results behavior; Player empty-library and sign-in states; Captain desktop/mobile grids, tabs, wizard selection/progress, and focus restoration; Studio density; invitation recovery; route metadata; immersive journal entry; console output; and document-width checks at the six required viewports.\r\n+\r\n Tall Tale Studio unit coverage asserts the complete 23-type block registry, schema metadata, required-field validation, provider dispatch, safe unknown-block behavior, normalization, conditions, variable mutation, route rate limits, slug rules, optimistic autosave tokens, and the helper verification envelope. Browser coverage proves the version-pinned player/Captain/helper path, helper heartbeat and revocation, completed catalog state, dnd-kit authoring affordance, responsive inspector dismissal, isolated block preview, published-version preview, copy-to-new-draft, and a media-rich create/upload/author/align/publish/play/artifact/history golden path. Database verification additionally proves that the additive seed has a current immutable version, two chapters, and a terminal block while retaining legacy campaign data. For a manual golden path: edit and preview the seeded tale, publish, start it from `/tales`, solve `lantern`, approve it from `/captain`, and complete the remaining blocks.\r\n \r\n Tall Tale Platform unit coverage adds lifecycle transition rules, role/resource authorization, and audit redaction. Its Chromium journey forks and publishes a Tale, atomically creates and accepts a secure invitation, denies cross-role access and missing Player CSRF, launches the crew, publishes a newer edition, proves the active playthrough remains pinned, completes it, validates the exact-version archive, persists pin/hide preferences, retries acceptance idempotently, and verifies replacement/revocation. A read-only mobile WebKit pass covers the reduced-motion gateway, responsive width, accessibility, and protected API denials. Validation also creates a legacy-shaped playthrough, runs the normal progress-preserving seed, and proves that ID, version, events, timestamps, membership, and reveal history survive backfill.\r\n@@ -40,3 +42,5 @@ Tall Tale Platform unit coverage adds lifecycle transition rules, role/resource\r\n Canonical journal unit coverage asserts the typed presentation schema, recursive Player secret removal, mode mapping, stable page IDs/parity, two-page spreads, chapter/current-objective indexes, StPageFlip revision refresh, queued turn locking, and distinct left/right geometry. === \"undefined\") {\n    onChange(true);\n    return () => undefined;\n  }\n  const observer = new IntersectionObserver(([entry]) => onChange(entry.isIntersecting), { rootMargin: \"100px\" });\n lottie: 0, pageFlip: 0, gsap: 0 };\nconst failures = new Set<string>();\n\nexport function changeMountedMetric(key: MetricKey, delta: number) {\n  counts[key] = Math.max(0, counts[key] + delta);\n null;\n}\n\nexport function isJournalInteractive(phase: JournalOpeningPhase) {\n  return phase === \"JOURNAL_READY\";\n}\n\nfunction abortError() {\n  return new DOMException(\"The journal opening was mode,\r\n    className = \"\",\r\n    initialPage = 0,\r\n    showCover = true,\r\n    playbackRate = 1,\r\n    revision = 0,\r\n    onPageChange,\r\n    onFlipStateChange,\r\n  },\r\n  ref,\r\n) {\r\n  const host = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\r\n  const source = useRef<HTMLDivElement>(null);\r\n  const instance = useRef<PageFlipInstance | null>(null);\r\n  const pendingPage = useRef<number | null>(null);\r\n  const [current, setCurrent] = useState(Math.min(initialPage, Math.max(0, pages.length - 1)));\r\n  const [orientation, setOrientation] = useState<\"portrait\" | \"landscape\">(\"landscape\");\r\n  const [flipState, setFlipState] = useState<\"folding\" | \"flipping\" | \"read\">(\"read\");\r\n  const [failed, setFailed] = useState(false);\r\n  const flipStateCallback = useRef(onFlipStateChange);\r\n  const signature = useMemo(() => pages.map((page) => page.id).join(\"|\"), [pages]);\r\n  const reduced = mode === \"reduced\";\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() 1);\r\n    setCurrent(next);\r\n    onPageChange?.(next);\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  const requestPage = (page: number, animated: boolean) => {\r\n    if (reduced) {\r\n      changePage(page);\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    if (flipState !== \"read\") {\r\n      pendingPage.current = page;\r\n      return;\r\n    }\r\n    if (animated) instance.current?.flip(page, \"top\");\r\n    else instance.current?.turnToPage(page);\r\n  };\r\n\r\n  useImperativeHandle(ref, () => ({\r\n    next: () => (reduced ? changePage(current + 1) : flipState === \"read\" && instance.current?.flipNext(\"top\")),\r\n    previous: () => (reduced ? changePage(current - 1) : flipState === \"read\" && instance.current?.flipPrev(\"top\")),\r\n    turnTo: (page) => requestPage(page, false),\r\n    flipTo: (page) => requestPage(page, true),\r\n    currentPage: () => (reduced ? current : (instance.current?.getCurrentPageIndex() ?? current)),\r\n    pageCount: () => (reduced ? pages.length : (instance.current?.getPageCount() ?? pages.length)),\r\n    orientation: () => (reduced ? \"portrait\" : (instance.current?.getOrientation() ?? orientation)),\r\n  }));\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const hostElement = host.current;\r\n    const sourceElement = source.current;\r\n    if (reduced || !hostElement || !sourceElement) return;\r\n    let disposed = false;\r\n    void import(\"page-flip\")\r\n      .then(({ PageFlip }) => {\r\n        if (disposed) return;\r\n        const book = new PageFlip(hostElement, {\r\n          width: 560,\r\n          height: 760,\r\n          size: \"stretch\",\r\n          minWidth: 300,\r\n          maxWidth: 720,\r\n          minHeight: 420,\r\n          maxHeight: 960,\r\n          showCover,\r\n          usePortrait: true,\r\n          autoSize: true,\r\n          drawShadow: true,\r\n          maxShadowOpacity: 0.45,\r\n          flippingTime: Math.round((mode === \"full\" ? 1100 : 620) / playbackRate),\r\n          swipeDistance: 24,\r\n          mobileScrollSupport: true,\r\n          startPage: Math.min(initialPage, pages.length - 1),\r\n        });\r\n        const clones = Array.from(sourceElement.children).map((node) => node.cloneNode(true) as HTMLElement);\r\n        book.loadFromHTML(clones);\r\n        book.on(\"flip\", (event) => {\r\n          const page = preventScroll: true }),\r\n          );\r\n        });\r\n        book.on(\"changeOrientation\", (event) => setOrientation(event.data as \"portrait\" | \"landscape\"));\r\n        book.on(\"changeState\", (event) => {\r\n          const state = event.data === \"flipping\" ? \"flipping\" : event.data === \"read\" ? \"read\" : \"folding\";\r\n          setFlipState(state);\r\n          flipStateCallback.current?.(state);\r\n          if (state === \"read\" && pendingPage.current !== null) {\r\n            const page = pendingPage.current;\r\n            pendingPage.current = null;\r\n            if (page !== book.getCurrentPageIndex()) book.flip(page, \"top\");\r\n          }\r\n        });\r\n        instance.current = book;\r\n        changeMountedMetric(\"pageFlip\", 1);\r\n      })\r\n      .catch(() => {\r\n        setFailed(true);\r\n        recordAssetFailure(\"page-flip\");\r\n      });\r\n    return () => {\r\n      disposed = true;\r\n      if Math.min(instance.current.getCurrentPageIndex(), pages.length - 1);\r\n    if (safeCurrent !== instance.current.getCurrentPageIndex()) instance.current.turnToPage(safeCurrent);\r\n  }, [pages.length, reduced, revision, signature]);\r\n\r\n  const pageNodes = pages.map((page, index) => (\r\n    <article\r\n      key={page.id}\r\n      className={`ft-page density-${page.density} page-side-${index % 2 === 0 ? \"right\" : \"left\"}`}\r\n      data-density={page.density}\r\n      data-page-index={index}\r\n      data-page-side={index % 2 === 0 ? \"right\" : \"left\"}\r\n      tabIndex={-1}\r\n      aria-label={page.label}\r\n    >\r\n      {page.content}\r\n    </article>\r\n  ));\r\n\r\n  if (reduced || failed) {\r\n    return (\r\n      <section\r\n        className={`page-flip-book className={`ft-page density-${pages[current]?.density ?? \"soft\"} page-side-${current % 2 === 0 ? \"right\" : \"left\"}`}\r\n            data-page-side={current % 2 === 0 ? \"right\" : \"left\"}\r\n          >\r\n            {pages[current]?.content}\r\n          </article>\r\n        </div>\r\n        <PageControls\r\n          current={current}\r\n          count={pages.length}\r\n <section\r\n      className={`page-flip-book orientation-${orientation} ${className}`}\r\n      data-animation-owner=\"st-page-flip\"\r\n      data-flip-state={flipState}\r\n      onKeyDown={(event) => {\r\n        if (flipState !== \"read\") return;\r\n        if (event.key === \"ArrowRight\" || event.key === \"PageDown\") instance.current?.flipNext(\"top\");\r\n        if (event.key === \"ArrowLeft\" || event.key === \"PageUp\") instance.current?.flipPrev(\"top\");\r\n      }}\r\n    >\r\n      <div ref={source} className=\"page-flip-source\" aria-hidden=\"true\" inert>\r\n CompanionNavigation, MobileNavigation } from \"./workspace/CompanionNavigation\";\r\nimport { FinaleChamber } from \"./workspace/FinaleChamber\";\r\nimport { JournalWorkspace } from \"./workspace/JournalWorkspace\";\r\nimport { ObjectiveNote } from \"./workspace/ObjectiveNote\";\r\nimport { ShipsLog } from \"./workspace/ShipsLog\";\r\nimport { SideQuestLedger } from \"./workspace/SideQuestLedger\";\r\nimport { TreasureAltar } from \"./workspace/TreasureAltar\";\r\nimport { companionViews, type CompanionView } from \"./workspace/types\";\r\nimport { VoyageChart } from \"./workspace/VoyageChart\";\r\n\r\nconst sceneByEvent: Partial<Record<ClientProgressEvent[\"type\"], AnimationSceneName>> = {\r\n  CHAPTER_RELEASED: \"chapter-release\",\r\n  CHAPTER_SOLVED: \"mark-solved\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_AWARDED: \"artifact-award\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_SILHOUETTE_REVEALED: \"artifact-award\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_CONNECTED: \"artifact-connection\",\r\n  MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED: \"map-reveal\",\r\n  MAP_ROUTE_REVEALED: \"route-draw\",\r\n  SIDE_QUEST_DISCOVERED: \"quest-discovery\",\r\n  SIDE_QUEST_UPDATED: \"quest-discovery\",\r\n  SIDE_QUEST_COMPLETED: \"quest-complete\",\r\n  JOURNAL_ANNOTATION_ADDED: \"log-entry\",\r\n  PLAYER_LOG_ENTRY_ADDED: \"log-entry\",\r\n  FINALE_TEASED: \"finale-tease\",\r\n  FINALE_REQUIREMENT_UPDATED: \"finale-requirement\",\r\n  CAMPAIGN_PAUSED: \"pause\",\r\n  CAMPAIGN_RESUMED: \"resume\",\r\n  STATE_REVERTED: \"undo\",\r\n};\r\n\r\nconst cueByEvent: Partial<Record<ClientProgressEvent[\"type\"], AudioCueName>> = {\r\n  CHAPTER_RELEASED: \"wax-crack\",\r\n  CHAPTER_SOLVED: \"stamp-impact\",\r\n  ARTIFACT_AWARDED: \"artifact-chime\",\r\n  MAP_LOCATION_REVEALED: \"compass-click\",\r\n  MAP_ROUTE_REVEALED: \"map-scratch\",\r\n  FINALE_TEASED: \"mechanism-hum\",\r\n  CAMPAIGN_PAUSED: \"pause-wind-down\",\r\n  STATE_REVERTED: \"undo-reverse\",\r\n};\r\n\r\nfunction deviceId() {\r\n  let id = localStorage.getItem(\"forever-device\");\r\n  if (!id) {\r\n    id = crypto.randomUUID();\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-device\", id);\r\n  }\r\n  return id;\r\n}\r\n\r\nfunction nextFrame() {\r\n  return new Promise<void>((resolve) => window.requestAnimationFrame(() => resolve()));\r\n}\r\n\r\nexport function PlayerExperience({ initialSnapshot }: { initialSnapshot: PublicSnapshot }) {\r\n  const root = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);\r\n  const [snapshot, setSnapshot] = useState(initialSnapshot);\r\n  const [view, setView] = useState<CompanionView>(\"journal\");\r\n  const [openingPhase, setOpeningPhase] = useState<JournalOpeningPhase>(\"ENTRY_IDLE\");\r\n  const [resettingJournal, setResettingJournal] = useState(false);\r\n  const [muted, setMuted] = useState(false);\r\n  const [volume, setVolumeState] = useState(0.4);\r\n  const [connection, setConnection] = useState<\"connecting\" | \"live\" | \"adrift\">(\"connecting\");\r\n  const [lastRelease, setLastRelease] = useState<ClientProgressEvent | null>(null);\r\n  const [activeEvent, setActiveEvent] = useState<ClientProgressEvent | null>(null);\r\n  const [selectedArtifact, setSelectedArtifact] = useState<string | null>(null);\r\n  const [inspectionOrigin, setInspectionOrigin] = useState<HTMLElement | null>(null);\r\n  const [objectiveOpen, setObjectiveOpen] = useState(false);\r\n  const [textScale, setTextScaleState] = useState(1);\r\n  const [texture, setTextureState] = useState(1);\r\n  const [openingSpeed, setOpeningSpeed] = useState<0.25 | 0.5 | 1>(1);\r\n  const eventChain = useRef<Promise<void>>(Promise.resolve());\r\n  const openingRun = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\r\n  const openingBusy = useRef(false);\r\n  const seenEvents = useRef(new Set<string>());\r\n  const latestSequence = useRef(initialSnapshot.sequence);\r\n  const audio = useRef(new AudioCuePlayer());\r\n  const { director, snapshot: animation } = useAnimationDirector();\r\n  const { mode, cycle } = useMotionMode();\r\n  const journalReady = isJournalInteractive(openingPhase);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const audioEngine = audio.current;\r\n    queueMicrotask(() => {\r\n      const savedMuted = localStorage.getItem(\"forever-muted\") === \"true\";\r\n      const savedVolume = Number(localStorage.getItem(\"forever-volume\") ?? 0.4);\r\n      setMuted(savedMuted);\r\n      setVolumeState(savedVolume);\r\n      setTextScaleState(Number(localStorage.getItem(\"forever-text-scale\") ?? 1));\r\n      setTextureState(Number(localStorage.getItem(\"forever-texture\") ?? 1));\r\n      audioEngine.setMuted(savedMuted);\r\n      audioEngine.setVolume(savedVolume);\r\n    });\r\n    return () => {\r\n      openingRun.current?.abort();\r\n      audioEngine.close();\r\n    };\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n  const refreshSnapshot = useCallback(async () => {\r\n    const response = await fetch(`/api/player/${initialSnapshot.campaign.slug}/snapshot`, { cache: \"no-store\" });\r\n    if (!response.ok) throw new Error(\"The latest voyage state could not be loaded.\");\r\n    const next = (await response.json()) as PublicSnapshot;\r\n    latestSequence.current = Math.max(latestSequence.current, next.sequence);\r\n    setSnapshot(next);\r\n    return next;\r\n  }, [initialSnapshot.campaign.slug]);\r\n\r\n  const completeEvent = useCallback(\r\n    async (event: ClientProgressEvent) => {\r\n      try {\r\n        await refreshSnapshot();\r\n        if (!event.id.includes(\":replay:\")) {\r\n          await fetch(`/api/player/${initialSnapshot.campaign.slug}/viewed`, {\r\n            method: \"POST\",\r\n            headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n            body: JSON.stringify({ eventId: event.id, deviceId: deviceId() }),\r\n          });\r\n        }\r\n      } catch {\r\n        setConnection(\"adrift\");\r\n      }\r\n    },\r\n    [initialSnapshot.campaign.slug, refreshSnapshot],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  const playEvent = useCallback(\r\n    async (event: ClientProgressEvent, replay = false) => {\r\n      if (!root.current) return;\r\n      const scene = sceneByEvent[event.type];\r\n      flushSync(() => {\r\n        if (event.type === \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\") setLastRelease(event);\r\n        setActiveEvent(event);\r\n      });\r\n      const cue = cueByEvent[event.type];\r\n      if (cue) audio.current.play(cue);\r\n      try {\r\n        if (scene)\r\n          await director.play(scene, {\r\n            root: root.current,\r\n            display: event.payload as Record<string, string | number | boolean>,\r\n            queue: true,\r\n          });\r\n      } catch {\r\n        // Presentation failure must not prevent reconciliation with authoritative state.\r\n      }\r\n      if (!replay) await completeEvent(event);\r\n      setActiveEvent(null);\r\n    },\r\n    [completeEvent, director],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const source = new EventSource(\r\n      `/api/player/${initialSnapshot.campaign.slug}/events?after=${latestSequence.current}`,\r\n    );\r\n    const offline = () => setConnection(\"adrift\");\r\n    const online = () => {\r\n      setConnection(\"connecting\");\r\n      void refreshSnapshot()\r\n        .then(() => setConnection(\"live\"))\r\n        .catch(() => setConnection(\"adrift\"));\r\n    };\r\n    source.onopen = () => setConnection(\"live\");\r\n    source.onerror = () => setConnection(\"adrift\");\r\n    source.addEventListener(\"progression\", (message) => {\r\n      const event = JSON.parse((message as MessageEvent).data) as ClientProgressEvent;\r\n      if (seenEvents.current.has(event.id)) return;\r\n      seenEvents.current.add(event.id);\r\n      latestSequence.current = Math.max(latestSequence.current, event.sequence);\r\n      eventChain.current = eventChain.current.then(() => playEvent(event)).catch(() => undefined);\r\n    });\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"offline\", offline);\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"online\", online);\r\n    return () => {\r\n      source.close();\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"offline\", offline);\r\n      window.removeEventListener(\"online\", online);\r\n    };\r\n  }, [initialSnapshot.campaign.slug, playEvent, refreshSnapshot]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const report = (disconnected = false) =>\r\n      fetch(`/api/player/${snapshot.campaign.slug}/presence`, {\r\n        method: \"POST\",\r\n        headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n        body: JSON.stringify({\r\n          deviceId: deviceId(),\r\n          route: `${location.pathname}#${view}`,\r\n          visibility: document.visibilityState,\r\n          acknowledgedSequence: snapshot.sequence,\r\n          disconnected,\r\n        }),\r\n        keepalive: disconnected,\r\n      }).catch(() => undefined);\r\n    void report();\r\n    const interval = window.setInterval(() => void report(), 20_000);\r\n    const visibility = () => void report();\r\n    document.addEventListener(\"visibilitychange\", visibility);\r\n    return () => {\r\n      window.clearInterval(interval);\r\n      document.removeEventListener(\"visibilitychange\", visibility);\r\n      void report(true);\r\n    };\r\n  }, [snapshot.campaign.slug, snapshot.sequence, view]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const readLocation = () => {\r\n      const params = new URLSearchParams(location.search);\r\n      const requested = params.get(\"section\") as CompanionView | null;\r\n      setView(requested && companionViews.some((item) => item.key === requested) ? requested : \"journal\");\r\n      if (process.env.NODE_ENV !== \"production\") {\r\n        const requestedSpeed = Number(params.get(\"journalSpeed\") ?? 1);\r\n        setOpeningSpeed(requestedSpeed === 0.25 || requestedSpeed === 0.5 ? requestedSpeed : 1);\r\n      }\r\n    };\r\n    readLocation();\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"popstate\", readLocation);\r\n    return () => window.removeEventListener(\"popstate\", readLocation);\r\n  }, []);\r\n\r\n\n---RESULT 2---\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 0.6 seconds\nOutput:\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    if (!journalReady) return;\r\n    const entries: Record<CompanionView, { contentType: string; contentKeys: string[] }> = {\r\n      journal: {\r\n        contentType: \"chapter\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.chapters.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => String(item.ordinal)),\r\n      },\r\n      chart: {\r\n        contentType: \"map\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.mapLocations.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key),\r\n      },\r\n      treasures: {\r\n        contentType: \"artifact\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.artifacts.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key),\r\n      },\r\n      quests: {\r\n        contentType: \"quest\",\r\n        contentKeys: snapshot.sideQuests.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key),\r\n      },\r\n      log: { contentType: \"log\", contentKeys: snapshot.log.filter((item) => item.unseen).map((item) => item.key) },\r\n      finale: { contentType: \"finale\", contentKeys: snapshot.finale.unseen ? [snapshot.finale.state] : [] },\r\n    };\r\n    const entry = entries[view];\r\n    if (!entry.contentKeys.length) return;\r\n    const controller = new AbortController();\r\n    void fetch(`/api/player/${snapshot.campaign.slug}/viewed`, {\r\n      method: \"POST\",\r\n      headers: { \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\" },\r\n      body: JSON.stringify(entry),\r\n      signal: controller.signal,\r\n    })\r\n      .then(() => refreshSnapshot())\r\n      .catch(() => undefined);\r\n    return () => controller.abort();\r\n  }, [journalReady, refreshSnapshot, snapshot, view]);\r\n\r\n  async function openJournal(forceFull = false) {\r\n    if (openingBusy.current || !root.current) return;\r\n    openingBusy.current = true;\r\n    const controller = new AbortController();\r\n    openingRun.current = controller;\r\n    audio.current.stopAll();\r\n    audio.current.unlock();\r\n    const advance = async (phase: JournalOpeningPhase, cue?: AudioCueName) => {\r\n      if (controller.signal.aborted || !root.current) throw new DOMException(\"Opening interrupted\", \"AbortError\");\r\n      flushSync(() => setOpeningPhase(phase));\r\n      if (cue) audio.current.play(cue);\r\n      await waitForJournalPhase(root.current, phase, mode, controller.signal);\r\n    };\r\n    try {\r\n      if (forceFull) {\r\n        flushSync(() => {\r\n          setResettingJournal(true);\r\n          setView(\"journal\");\r\n          setOpeningPhase(\"ENTRY_IDLE\");\r\n        });\r\n        await nextFrame();\r\n        await nextFrame();\r\n        flushSync(() => setResettingJournal(false));\r\n      }\r\n      await advance(\"ENTRY_ACTIVATED\", \"ocean-rise\");\r\n      const key = `forever-intro:${snapshot.campaign.slug}`;\r\n      const first = forceFull || sessionStorage.getItem(key) !== \"seen\";\r\n      try {\r\n        await director.play(first ? \"first-arrival\" : \"session-reentry\", { root: root.current, queue: false });\r\n      } catch {\r\n        // The physical state machine remains authoritative if the atmospheric prelude is unavailable.\r\n      }\r\n      await advance(\"CLOSED_BOOK_REVEAL\");\r\n      await advance(\"LATCH_RELEASING\", \"brass-latch\");\r\n      await advance(\"COVER_OPENING\", \"wood-creak\");\r\n      await advance(\"SEALED_PAGE_REVEAL\", \"seal-pressure\");\r\n      await advance(\"SEAL_BREAKING\", \"wax-crack\");\r\n      await advance(\"BOOK_SETTLING\", \"paper-flutter\");\r\n      await advance(\"JOURNAL_READY\");\r\n      sessionStorage.setItem(key, \"seen\");\r\n    } catch (cause) {\r\n      if (!(cause instanceof DOMException && cause.name === \"AbortError\")) {\r\n        flushSync(() => setOpeningPhase(\"JOURNAL_READY\"));\r\n      }\r\n    } finally {\r\n      if (openingRun.current === controller) openingRun.current = null;\r\n      openingBusy.current = false;\r\n    }\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  function skipJournalOpening() {\r\n    sessionStorage.setItem(`forever-intro:${snapshot.campaign.slug}`, \"seen\");\r\n    openingRun.current?.abort();\r\n    director.skip();\r\n    audio.current.stopAll();\r\n    flushSync(() => setOpeningPhase(\"JOURNAL_READY\"));\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  function navigate(next: CompanionView) {\r\n    setView(next);\r\n    const url = new URL(location.href);\r\n    url.searchParams.set(\"section\", next);\r\n    history.pushState({}, \"\", url);\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  function toggleMute() {\r\n    setMuted((value) => {\r\n      const next = !value;\r\n      localStorage.setItem(\"forever-muted\", String(next));\r\n      audio.current.setMuted(next);\r\n      return next;\r\n    });\r\n  }\r\n  function setVolume(value: number) {\r\n    setVolumeState(value);\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-volume\", String(value));\r\n    audio.current.setVolume(value);\r\n  }\r\n  function setTextScale(value: number) {\r\n    setTextScaleState(value);\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-text-scale\", String(value));\r\n  }\r\n  function setTexture(value: number) {\r\n    setTextureState(value);\r\n    localStorage.setItem(\"forever-texture\", String(value));\r\n  }\r\n\r\n  const payloadObjective =\r\n    activeEvent?.type === \"CHAPTER_RELEASED\" &&\r\n    [\r\n      \"ink-objective\",\r\n      \"ink-riddle\",\r\n      \"map\",\r\n      \"active\",\r\n      \"content-readable\",\r\n      \"interaction-restored\",\r\n      \"scene-complete\",\r\n    ].includes(animation.label)\r\n      ? String(activeEvent.payload.objective ?? \"\")\r\n      : null;\r\n  const objective = payloadObjective || snapshot.chapter.objective || \"Await the captain's signal.\";\r\n  const inspectedArtifact = snapshot.artifacts.find((artifact) => artifact.key === selectedArtifact);\r\n  const animationSequence =\r\n    animation.scene === \"first-arrival\"\r\n      ? \"firstArrival\"\r\n      : animation.scene === \"session-reentry\"\r\n        ? \"reentry\"\r\n        : (animation.scene ?? \"dormant\");\r\n\r\n  return (\r\n    <main\r\n      ref={root}\r\n      className={`voyage-shell stage-${animation.label} view-${view}${resettingJournal ? \" journal-resetting\" : \"\"}`}\r\n      data-cinematic-sequence={animationSequence}\r\n      data-journal-phase={openingPhase}\r\n      data-journal-speed={openingSpeed}\r\n      data-motion-mode={mode}\r\n      style={{ \"--player-text-scale\": textScale, \"--texture-opacity\": texture } as React.CSSProperties}\r\n    >\r\n      <div className=\"ocean-depth\" data-scene-part=\"workspace-light\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"sky\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"horizon\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"ocean\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"fog-back\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n        <div data-scene-part=\"fog-front\" data-gsap-owned />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div className=\"player-lantern\" data-scene-part=\"lantern\" data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\">\r\n        <i />\r\n        <b />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div\r\n        className=\"persistent-interface\"\r\n        data-opening-actor=\"persistent-interface\"\r\n        aria-hidden={!journalReady}\r\n        inert={!journalReady ? true : undefined}\r\n      >\r\n        <CompanionHeader\r\n          connection={connection}\r\n          muted={muted}\r\n          volume={volume}\r\n          mode={mode}\r\n          textScale={textScale}\r\n          texture={texture}\r\n          canReplay={Boolean(lastRelease)}\r\n          toggleMute={toggleMute}\r\n          setVolume={setVolume}\r\n          cycleMotion={cycle}\r\n          setTextScale={setTextScale}\r\n          setTexture={setTexture}\r\n          replay={() =>\r\n            lastRelease && void playEvent({ ...lastRelease, id: `${lastRelease.id}:replay:${Date.now()}` }, true)\r\n          }\r\n        />\r\n        <CompanionNavigation view={view} unseen={snapshot.unseen} navigate={navigate} />\r\n      </div>\r\n      <div className=\"persistent-mobile-interface\" aria-hidden={!journalReady} inert={!journalReady ? true : undefined}>\r\n        <MobileNavigation view={view} unseen={snapshot.unseen} navigate={navigate} />\r\n      </div>\r\n      {(openingPhase === \"ENTRY_IDLE\" || openingPhase === \"ENTRY_ACTIVATED\") && (\r\n        <div className=\"journal-opening\">\r\n          <button className=\"wax-open\" onClick={() => void openJournal()}>\r\n            <span>F</span>\r\n            <strong>Open the journal</strong>\r\n            <small>Sound begins only after you choose</small>\r\n          </button>\r\n        </div>\r\n      )}\r\n      <div className=\"physical-workspace\" aria-hidden={!journalReady} inert={!journalReady ? true : undefined}>\r\n        <AnimatePresence mode=\"wait\" initial={false}>\r\n          <motion.div\r\n            key={view}\r\n            className=\"section-transition\"\r\n            variants={sectionVariants(mode)}\r\n            initial=\"initial\"\r\n            animate=\"enter\"\r\n            exit=\"exit\"\r\n            onAnimationComplete={(definition) => {\r\n              if (definition === \"enter\" && journalReady) {\r\n                document.querySelector<HTMLElement>(\"[data-section-heading]\")?.focus({ preventScroll: true });\r\n              }\r\n            }}\r\n            data-animation-owner=\"motion\"\r\n          >\r\n            {view === \"journal\" && (\r\n              <JournalWorkspace\r\n                snapshot={snapshot}\r\n                mode={mode}\r\n                activeEvent={activeEvent}\r\n                openingPhase={openingPhase}\r\n                interactive={journalReady}\r\n                playbackRate={openingSpeed}\r\n                onPageTurn={() => audio.current.play(\"page-turn\")}\r\n              />\r\n            )}\r\n            {view === \"chart\" && <VoyageChart snapshot={snapshot} mode={mode} />}\r\n            {view === \"treasures\" && (\r\n              <TreasureAltar\r\n                snapshot={snapshot}\r\n                inspect={(key, element) => {\r\n                  setInspectionOrigin(element);\r\n                  setSelectedArtifact(key);\r\n                }}\r\n              />\r\n            )}\r\n            {view === \"quests\" && <SideQuestLedger snapshot={snapshot} mode={mode} />}\r\n            {view === \"log\" && <S…2464 tokens truncated…ateway-content\" aria-labelledby=\"gateway-title\">\n        <p className=\"eyebrow\" data-scene-part=\"arrival-copy\" data-gsap-owned>\n          The chart table is ready\n        </p>\n        <h1 id=\"gateway-title\" data-scene-part=\"title\" data-gsap-owned>\n          Choose your place in the Tale\n        </h1>\n        <p data-scene-part=\"arrival-copy\" data-gsap-owned>\n          One living collection of adventures opens three different ways. Your choice opens a sign-in path; your account\n          and voyage decide what you may see.\n        </p>\n        <div\n          className=\"role-object-grid\"\n          aria-label=\"Tall Tale roles\"\n          aria-live=\"polite\"\n          data-scene-part=\"arrival-action\"\n          data-gsap-owned\n        >\n          {roles.map((role) => {\n            const next = continuation(role);\n            const current = status?.[role.id];\n            return (\n              <motion.article key={role.id} className={`role-object-card role-${role.id}`} {...pressable(mode)}>\n                <div className={`role-object ${role.object}`} data-role-object data-gsap-owned aria-hidden=\"true\">\n                  <i />\n                  <b />\n                  <span />\n                </div>\n                <p className=\"card-kicker\">{current?.authenticated ? \"Session remembered\" : \"Adventure role\"}</p>\n                <h2>{role.title}</h2>\n                <p>{role.copy}</p>\n                {role.id === \"captain\" && status?.captain.authenticated && (\n                  <small>\n                    {status.captain.waitingPlayers ?? 0} {status.captain.waitingPlayers === 1 ? \"Player\" : \"Players\"}{\" \"}\n                    awaiting launch\n                  </small>\n                )}\n                {role.id === \"creator\" && status?.creator.authenticated && status.creator.recentDraft && (\n                  <small>Recent draft: {status.creator.recentDraft.title}</small>\n                )}\n                <Link className=\"role-entry\" href={next.href} aria-describedby={`role-${role.id}-description`}>\n                  {next.label}\n                </Link>\n                <span id={`role-${role.id}-description`} className=\"sr-only\">\n                  {role.copy}\n                </span>\n              </motion.article>\n            );\n          })}\n        </div>\n        <Link className=\"invitation-code-link\" href=\"/player/sign-in#invitation-code\">\n          Have an invitation code?\n        </Link>\n      </section>\n      <div className=\"landing-controls\" aria-label=\"Gateway presentation controls\">\n        <button onClick={replay} disabled={snapshot.isPlaying}>\n          Replay gateway\n        </button>\n        {snapshot.isPlaying && snapshot.label !== \"dark-sea\" && (\n          <button onClick={() => director.skip()}>Skip arrival</button>\n        )}\n        <button onClick={cycle} aria-label={`Motion: ${mode}. Change motion setting`}>\n          {mode} motion\n        </button>\n      </div>\n      <AnimationTestButton />\n    </main>\n  );\n}\n\r\n\"use client\";\n\nimport { useRouter } from \"next/navigation\";\nimport { useRef, useState } from \"react\";\nimport { motion } from \"motion/react\";\nimport { lottieAssets } from \"@/animation/assets/lottie-contracts\";\nimport { riveAssets } from \"@/animation/assets/rive-contracts\";\nimport { useAnimationDirector } from \"@/animation/director/useAnimationDirector\";\nimport { useMotionMode } from \"@/animation/motion/useMotionMode\";\nimport { pressable } from \"@/animation/motion/variants\";\nimport { LottieEffect } from \"@/components/animation/LottieEffect\";\nimport { RiveStatefulObject }      <p className=\"eyebrow\">Primary story artifact</p>\n          <h2 id=\"journal-heading\">The Voyage Journal</h2>\n        </div>\n        <p>Selectable parchment, physical leaves, and only the    {page.title && <h3>{page.title}</h3>}\n      {page.body && <p className=\"journal-prose\">{page.body}</p>}\n      {page.objective && (\n        <div className=\"page-objective\">\n          <span>Present course</span>\n          <strong>{page.objective}</strong>\n        </div>\n      )}\n      {page.riddle && (\n        <blockquote>\n          {page.riddle.split(\"\\n\").map((line) => location])),\n    [snapshot.mapLocations],\n  );\n  return (\n    <section\n      className=\"physical-section voyage-chart-section\"\n      aria-labelledby=\"chart-heading\"\n      data-section-heading\n      tabIndex={-1}\n    >\n      <header className=\"section-masthead\">\n        <div>\n          <p className=\"eyebrow\">Released bearings only</p>\n          <h2 className=\"chart-instrument-bar\" aria-label=\"Map controls\">\n        <button onClick={() => setScale((value) => Math.min(1.8, value + 0.2))}>Zoom in</button>\n        <button onClick={() => setScale((value) => Math.max(0.8, value - 0.2))}>Zoom out</button>\n        <button\n          onClick={() => {\n            setScale(1);\n            setPan({ x: 0, y: 0 });\n          }}\n        >\n          Reset chart\n        </button>\n        <span>Zoom {Math.round(scale * 100)}%</span>\n      </div>\n      <div\n        className=\"chart-viewport\"\n        tabIndex={0}\n        aria-label=\"Interactive voyage chart. Use arrow keys to pan.\"\n        onKeyDown={(event) => {\n          const amount = 28;\n          if (event.key === \"ArrowLeft\") setPan((p) => ({ ...p, x: p.x + amount }));\n          if (event.key === \"ArrowRight\") setPan((p) => ({ ...p, x: p.x - amount }));\n          if (event.key === \"ArrowUp\") setPan((p) => ({ ...p, y: p.y + amount }));\n          if (event.key === \"ArrowDown\") setPan((p) => ({ ...p, y: p.y - amount }));\n        }}\n      >\n        <motion.div\n          className=\"illustrated-chart\"\n          animate={{ scale, x: pan.x, y: pan.y }}\n          transition={mode === \"reduced\" ? (document.activeElement as HTMLElement | null);\n    node?.querySelector<HTMLButtonElement>(\"button\")?.focus();\n    const keydown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\n      if (event.key === \"Escape\") close();\n      if (event.key !== \"Tab\" || !node) return;\n      const focusable = Array.from(\n        node.querySelectorAll<HTMLElement>(\"button, [href], input, select, textarea, [tabindex]:not([tabindex='-1'])\"),\n      );\n      if (!focusable.length) return;\n      const first = focusable[0];\n      const last = focusable.at(-1)!;\n      if (event.shiftKey && Short code ${invitation.shortCode}.`}\n          />\n          <div>\n            <p className=\"eyebrow\">Shown once after creation</p>\n            <h3>{invitation.recipientName}</h3>\n            <code>{invitation.shortCode}</code>\n            <p>{invitation.message}</p>\n            <div>\n              <button onClick={() => void copy(invitation, invitation.link)}>Copy secure link</button>\n              <button onClick={() => void copy(invitation, invitation.message)}>Copy message</button>\n            </div>\n            <small>Expires {new Date(invitation.expiresAt).toLocaleString()}</small>\n          </div>\n        </article>\n      ))}\n    </div>\n  );\n}\n\r\n\n---RESULT 4---\nExit code: 0\nWall time: 0.4 seconds\nOutput:\n\"use client\";\n\nimport Link from \"next/link\";\nimport { useEffect, useMemo, useState } from \"react\";\n\ntype TaleCard = {\n  id: string;\n  slug: string;\n  title: string;\n  subtitle: string | null;\n  shortDescription: string | null;\n  status: string;\n  visibility: string;\n  savedAt: string;\n  validationState: string;\n  latestVersion: string | null;\n  sessionCount: number;\n  assetCount: number;\n};\n\nexport function StudioHome({ authenticated }: { authenticated: boolean }) {\n  const [tales, setTales] = useState<TaleCard[]>([]);\n  const [csrf, setCsrf] = useState(\"\");\n  const [query, setQuery] = useState(\"\");\n  const [sort, setSort] = useState(\"recent\");\n  const [error, setError] = useState(\"\");\n  const [busy, setBusy] = useState(\"\");\n\n  async function load() {\n    const response = await fetch(\"/api/studio/tales\", { cache: \"no-store\" });\n    const body = (await response.json()) as { tales?: TaleCard[]; csrfToken?: string; error?: string };\n    if (!response.ok) return setError(body.error ?? \"The Studio ledger could not be opened.\");\n    setTales(body.tales ?? (\n        <section className=\"studio-empty\">\n          <span aria-hidden=\"true\">âœ¦</span>\n          <h2>No Tall Tales on this chart</h2>\n          <p>Create the first editable voyage, or clear the current search.</p>\n          <Link className=\"brass-button\" href=\"/studio/tales/new\">\n            Create a Tall Tale\n          </Link>\n        </section>\n      ) : (\n        <section className=\"tale-card-grid\" aria-label=\"Tall Tales\">\n          {visible.map((tale) => (\n            <article className={`tale-studio-card status-${tale.status.toLowerCase()}`} key={tale.id}>\n              <div className=\"tale-card-stamp\">{tale.status.replaceAll(\"_\", \" \")}</div>\n              <p className=\"card-kicker\">\n                {tale.visibility.toLocaleLowerCase()} Â·{\" \"}\n                {tale.latestVersion ? SceneTimeline } from \"../core/animation-types\";\nimport { sceneRegistry } from \"../director/scene-registry\";\n\nfunction context(mode: MotionMode = \"full\") {\n  const cleanups: Array<() => void> = [];\n  const root = document.createElement(\"main\");\n  const partNames = [\n    \"sky\",\n    \"stars\",\n    \"moon\",\n    \"horizon\",\n    \"fog-back\",\n    \"fog-front\",\n    \"ocean\",\n    \"ship\",\n    \"title\",\n    \"emblem\",\n    \"arrival-copy\",\n    \"arrival-action\",\n    \"nautical-border\",\n    \"invitation-ink\",\n    \"seal\",\n    \"ribbon\",\n    \"invitation\",\n    \"seal-crack\",\n    \"seal-fragment\",\n    \"lantern\",\n    \"lock\",\n    \"door-bolt\",\n    \"cabin-door\",\n    \"chart-room-light\",\n    \"login-ledger\",\n    \"journal-clasp\",\n    \"journal-cover\",\n    \"page-dust\",\n    \"workspace-light\",\n    \"peripheral\",\n    \"journal-stage\",\n    \"sealed-parchment\",\n    \"ink-heading\",\n    \"ink-story\",\n    \"ink-objective\",\n    \"ink-riddle\",\n    \"route-path\",\n    \"page-light\",\n    \"map-fog\",\n    \"map-marker-new\",\n    \"artifact-connection-path\",\n    \"artifact-reveal\",\n    \"artifact-light\",\n    \"artifact-engraving\",\n    \"quest-note-new\",\n    \"red-thread\",\n    \"quest-stamp\",\n    \"log-entry-new\",\n    \"log-symbol-new\",\n    \"finale-ring-outer\",\n    \"finale-ring-inner\",\n    \"finale-light-path\",\n    \"living-idle\",\n    \"command-light\",\n    \"blank-page\",\n    \"solved-stamp\",\n    \"undo-mark\",\n  ];\n  root.innerHTML = partNames.map((name) => `<div data-scene-part=\"${name}\">Safe development text</div>`).join(\"\");\n  const value: SceneBuildContext = {\n    root,\n    mode,\n    sceneName: \"first-arrival\",\n    display: {},\n    emitLabel: () => undefined,\n    addCleanup: (cleanup) => cleanups.push(cleanup),\n  };\n  return { value, cleanups };\n}\n\nconst expectLabels = (timeline: SceneTimeline, labels: string[]) => {\n  for (const label of labels) expect(timeline.labels).toHaveProperty(label);\n};\n\ndescribe(\"GSAP scene builders\", () => {\n  it.each([\"full\", \"gentle\", \"reduced\"] as const)(\"builds every scene in %s mode without starting it\", (mode) => {\n    for (const definition of Object.values(sceneRegistry)) {\n      const built = context(mode);\n      built.value.sceneName = definition.name;\n      const timelines = [\n        definition.buildOpening(built.value),\n        definition.buildSuccess(built.value),\n        definition.buildFailure?.(built.value),\n        definition.buildIdle?.(built.value),\n      ].filter(Boolean) as SceneTimeline[];\n      for (const timeline of timelines) {\n        expect(timeline.paused()).toBe(true);\n        expect(timeline.labels).toHaveProperty(\"scene-start\");\n        timeline.kill();\n      }\n      built.cleanups.reverse().forEach((cleanup) => cleanup());\n    }\n  });\n\n  it(\"preserves semantic checkpoints for the arrival, access, and flagship chapter ceremony\", () => {\n    const arrival = context();\n    expectLabels(sceneRegistry[\"first-arrival\"].buildOpening(arrival.value), [\n      \"dark-sea\",\n      \"tide-arrives\",\n      \"title-written\",\n      \"voyage-materializes\",\n      \"physical-open\",\n      \"await-server\",\n    ]);\n\n    const access = context();\n    expectLabels(sceneRegistry[\"player-access\"].buildSuccess(access.value), [\n      \"success-branch\",\n      \"seal-released\",\n      \"invitation-unfolds\",\n      \"content-readable\",\n    ]);\n\n    const chapter = context();\n    expectLabels(sceneRegistry[\"chapter-release\"].buildSuccess(chapter.value), [\n      \"seal\",\n      \"parchment\",\n      \"ink-heading\",\n      \"ink-story\",\n      \"ink-objective\",\n      \"ink-riddle\",\n      \"map\",\n      \"active\",\n      \"content-readable\",\n      \"scene-complete\",\n    ]);\n    chapter.cleanups.reverse().forEach((cleanup) => cleanup());\n  });\n\n  it(\"provides truthful failure checkpoints for authenticated and GM operations\", () => {\n    const access = context();\n    expectLabels(sceneRegistry[\"player-access\"].buildFailure!(access.value), [\"failure-branch\", \"lock-rejected\"]);\n    const prepare = context();\n    expectLabels(sceneRegistry[\"prepare-chapter\"].buildFailure!(prepare.value), [\"failure-branch\", \"ink-receding\"]);\n  });\n});\n\r\nimport { describe, expect, it } from \"vitest\";\nimport { sceneNames } from \"../core/animation-types\";\nimport { sceneRegistry } from \"./scene-registry\";\n\ndescribe(\"scene registry\", () => {\n  it(\"contains one typed definition for every public scene name\", () => {\n    expect(Object.keys(sceneRegistry).sort()).toEqual([...sceneNames].sort());\n  });\n\n  it(\"gives every scene real opening and success timeline builders\", () => {\n    for (const definition of Object.values(sceneRegistry)) {\n      expect(definition.name).toBeTruthy();\n      expect(definition.buildOpening).toBeTypeOf(\"function\");\n      expect(definition.buildSuccess).toBeTypeOf(\"function\");\n    }\n  });\n});\n\r\nGet-Content : Cannot find path "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "---RESULT 4---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "Exit code: 0\nWall time: 1.3 seconds\nOutput:\nimport { describe, expect, it } from \"vitest\";\nimport { isJournalInteractive, journalOpeningPhases, nextJournalOpeningPhase } from \"./opening-machine\";\n\ndescribe(\"journal opening state machine\", () => {\n  it(\"advances through the complete physical sequence in one direction\", () => {\n    const visited: Array<(typeof journalOpeningPhases)[number]> = [journalOpeningPhases[0]];\n    let next = nextJournalOpeningPhase(visited[0]);\n    while (next) {\n      visited.push(next);\n      next = nextJournalOpeningPhase(next);\n    }\n    expect(visited).toEqual(journalOpeningPhases);\n  });\n\n  it(\"does not enable journal interaction before the book settles\", () => {\n    expect(journalOpeningPhases.slice(0, -1).every((phase) => !isJournalInteractive(phase))).toBe(true);\n    expect(isJournalInteractive(\"JOURNAL_READY\")).toBe(true);\n    expect(nextJournalOpeningPhase(\"JOURNAL_READY\")).toBeNull();\n  });\n});\n\r\nimport { cleanup, fireEvent, render, screen, waitFor } from \"@testing-library/react\";\nimport { createRef } from \"react\";\nimport { renderToString } from \"react-dom/server\";\nimport { afterEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from \"vitest\";\nimport { PageFlipBook, type FlipBookPage, type PageFlipBookHandle } from \"./PageFlipBook\";\n\nconst calls = vi.hoisted(() => ({\n  load: vi.fn(),\n  update: vi.fn(),\n  destroy: vi.fn(),\n  next: vi.fn(),\n  previous: vi.fn(),\n  turnTo: vi.fn(),\n  flipTo: vi.fn(),\n  constructors: vi.fn(),\n  instance: null as null | { current: number; handlers: Map<string, (event: { data: number | string }) => void> },\n}));\n\nvi.mock(\"page-flip\", () => ({\n  PageFlip: class {\n    current = 0;\n    handlers = new Map<string, (event: { data: number | string }) => void>();\n    constructor(host: HTMLElement, options: { flippingTime: number }) {\n      calls.constructors(host, options);\n      calls.instance = this;\n    }\n    loadFromHTML = calls.load;\n    updateFromHtml = calls.update;\n    destroy = calls.destroy;\n    flipNext = calls.next;\n    flipPrev = calls.previous;\n    turnToPage = calls.turnTo.mockImplementation((page: number) => (this.current = page));\n    flip = calls.flipTo.mockImplementation((page: number) => (this.current = page));\n    getCurrentPageIndex = () => this.current;\n    getPageCount = () => 3;\n    getOrientation = () => \"landscape\" as const;\n    on = (name: string, callback: (event: { data: number | string }) => void) => this.handlers.set(name, callback);\n  },\n}));\n\nconst pages: FlipBookPage[] = [\n  { id: \"one\", density: \"hard\", label: \"Cover\", content: <h2>Cover</h2> },\n  { id: \"two\", density: \"soft\", label: \"Story\", content: <p>Story</p> },\n];\n\ndescribe(\"PageFlipBook\", () => {\n  afterEach(() => {\n    cleanup();\n    vi.clearAllMocks();\n    calls.instance = null;\n  });\n\n  it(\"does not initialize the client-only runtime during server rendering\", () => {\n    expect(renderToString(<PageFlipBook pages={pages} mode=\"full\" />)).toContain(\"page-flip-source\");\n    expect(calls.constructors).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n  });\n\n  it(\"loads HTML once, updates the existing instance, and destroys on unmount\", async () => {\n    const { rerender, unmount } = render(<PageFlipBook pages={pages} mode=\"full\" />);\n    await waitFor(() => expect(calls.load).toHaveBeenCalledOnce());\n    rerender(\n      <PageFlipBook\n        pages={[...pages, { id: \"three\", density: \"soft\", label: \"Back\", content: <p>Back</p> }]}\n        mode=\"full\"\n      />,\n    );\n    await waitFor(() => expect(calls.update).toHaveBeenCalled());\n    expect(calls.constructors).toHaveBeenCalledOnce();\n    unmount();\n    expect(calls.destroy).toHaveBeenCalledOnce();\n  });\n\n  it(\"refreshes same-page content when the canonical session revision changes\", async () => {\n    const { rerender } = render(<PageFlipBook pages={pages} mode=\"full\" revision={1} />);\n    await waitFor(() => expect(calls.load).toHaveBeenCalledOnce());\n    rerender(<PageFlipBook pages={pages} mode=\"full\" revision={2} />);\n    await waitFor(() => expect(calls.update).toHaveBeenCalledOnce());\n  });\n\n  it(\"supports keyboard turns, event-driven page/orientation state, and imperative navigation\", async () => {\n    const ref = createRef<PageFlipBookHandle>();\n    const { container } = render(<PageFlipBook ref={ref} pages={pages} mode=\"full\" />);\n    await waitFor(() => expect(calls.instance).not.toBeNull());\n    const book = container.querySelector<HTMLElement>(\".page-flip-book\")!;\n    fireEvent.keyDown(book, { key: \"ArrowRight\" });\n    fireEvent.keyDown(book, { key: \"PageUp\" });\n    expect(calls.next).toHaveBeenCalledOnce();\n    expect(calls.previous).toHaveBeenCalledOnce();\n\n    calls.instance!.handlers.get(\"flip\")?.({ data: 1 });\n    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByText(\"Page 2 of 2\")).toBeVisible());\n    calls.instance!.handlers.get(\"changeOrientation\")?.({ data: \"portrait\" });\n    await waitFor(() => expect(book).toHaveClass(\"orientation-portrait\"));\n\n    ref.current?.turnTo(1);\n    ref.current?.flipTo(0);\n    expect(calls.turnTo).toHaveBeenCalledWith(1);\n    expect(calls.flipTo).toHaveBeenCalledWith(0, \"top\");\n    expect(ref.current?.pageCount()).toBe(3);\n    expect(ref.current?.orientation()).toBe(\"landscape\");\n  });\n\n  it(\"queues one programmatic turn while a physical page is already moving\", async () => {\n    const ref = createRef<PageFlipBookHandle>();\n    render(<PageFlipBook ref={ref} pages={pages} mode=\"full\" />);\n    await waitFor(() => expect(calls.instance).not.toBeNull());\n    calls.instance!.handlers.get(\"changeState\")?.({ data: \"flipping\" });\n    await waitFor(() => expect(screen.getByRole(\"button\", { name: \"Next journal page\" })).toBeDisabled());\n    ref.current?.flipTo(1);\n    expect(calls.flipTo).not.toHaveBeenCalled();\n    calls.instance!.handlers.get(\"changeState\")?.({ data: \"read\" ## Assistant
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T16:37:28.992Z

The static audit has now verified the central failure pattern: event scenes are scoped to the entire Player root, not to a dedicated visible host. As a result, the same generic scene-part names can match hidden PageFlip source nodes, StPageFlip clones, the active section, and temporary overlays simultaneously. Several scenes can also “complete” with zero targets because no required-target or visibility contract exists. I’m preparing the isolated baseline runtime next; no production source is being changed.
