setMode: (mode: MotionMode) => void }) {\n  const { director, snapshot } = useAnimationDirector();\n  return (\n    <section className=\"animation-controls\" aria-label=\"Animation playback controls\">\n      <div className=\"animation-readout\">\n        <span>{snapshot.scene ?? \"No scene\"}</span>\n        <strong>{snapshot.label}</strong>\n        <output>{Math.round(snapshot.progress * 100)}%</output>\n      </div>\n      <div className=\"transport-controls\">\n        <button\n          onClick={() => (snapshot.isPaused ? director.resume() : director.pause())}\n          disabled={!snapshot.isPlaying}\n        >\n          {snapshot.isPaused ? \"Resume\" : \"Pause\"}\n        </button>\n        <button onClick={() => director.seek(0)} disabled={!snapshot.isPlaying}>\n          Restart\n        </button>\n        <button onClick={() => director.skip()} disabled={!snapshot.isPlaying}>\n          Skip\n        </button>\n        <button onClick={() => director.reverse()} disabled={!snapshot.isPlaying}>\n          Reverse\n        </button>\n      </div>\n      <label>\n        Timeline{\" \"}\n        <input\n          aria-label=\"Timeline scrubber\"\n          type=\"range\"\n          min=\"0\"\n          max=\"1\"\n          step=\"0.01\"\n          value={snapshot.progress}\n          onChange={(event) => director.seek(Number(event.target.value))}\n        />\n      </label>\n      <label>\n        Speed{\" \"}\n        <select value={snapshot.speed} onChange={(event) => director.setSpeed(Number(event.target.value))}>\n          {[0.25, 0.5, 1, 1.5, 2].map((speed) => (\n            <option key={speed} value={speed}>\n              {speed}x\n            </option>\n          ))}\n        </select>\n      </label>\n      <fieldset>\n        <legend>Motion</legend>\n        {([\"full\", \"gentle\", \"reduced\"] as MotionMode[]).map((item) => (\n          <button key={item} aria-pressed={mode === item} onClick={() => setMode(item)}>\n            {item}\n          </button>\n        ))}\n      </fieldset>\n    </section>\n  );\n}\n\r\n      <div className=\"demo-page cover\">\r\n        <strong>Return by moonlight</strong>\r\n      </div>\r\n    ),\r\n  },\r\n];\r\n\r\nexport function AnimationShowcase() {\r\n  const root = useRef<HTMLElement>(null);\r\n  const lottie = useRef<LottieEffectHandle>(null);\r\n  const book = useRef<PageFlipBookHandle>(null);\r\n  const trailerCancelled = useRef(false);\r\n  const { director, snapshot } = useAnimationDirector();\r\n  const { mode, setMode } = useMotionMode();\r\n  const [selected, setSelected] = useState(\"arrival\");\r\n  const [library, setLibrary] = useState<Library>(\"all\");\r\n  const [errors, setErrors] = useState<string[]>([]);\r\n  const [assetStatus, setAssetStatus] = useState<Record<string, string>>({});\r\n  const [riveInputs, setRiveInputs] = useState<RiveRuntimeInput[]>([]);\r\n  const [riveSignal, setRiveSignal] = useState<RiveSignal>();\r\n  const [trailerPlaying, setTrailerPlaying] = useState(false);\r\n  const [trailerCard, setTrailerCard] = useState(\"Animation architecture ready\");\r\n  const [runtimeEpoch, setRuntimeEpoch] = useState(0);\r\n  const current = demos.find((demo) => demo.id === selected) ?? demos[0];\r\n  const visibleDemos = useMemo(\r\n    () => (library === \"all\" ? demos : demos.filter((demo) => demo.libraries.includes(library))),\r\n    [library],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  const play = useCallback(\r\n    async (demo = current) => {\r\n      if (!root.current) return;\r\n      try {\r\n        await director.play(demo.scene, {\r\n          root: root.current,\r\n          queue: false,\r\n          operation: demo.operation\r\n            ? async () => {\r\n                if (demo.operation === \"failure\") throw new Error(\"Deterministic showcase rejection\");\r\n                return { safe: true };\r\n              }\r\n            : undefined,\r\n        });\r\n        if (demo.scene === \"programmatic-page-flip\") book.current?.flipTo(2);\r\n      } catch (cause) {\r\n        if (demo.operation !== \"failure\")\r\n          setErrors((items) => [...items, cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"Unknown showcase error\"]);\r\n      }\r\n    },\r\n    [current, director],\r\n  );\r\n\r\n  const playTrailer = useCallback(async () => {\r\n    if (!root.current || trailerPlaying) return;\r\n    trailerCancelled.current = false;\r\n    setTrailerPlaying(true);\r\n    const originalSpeed = snapshot.speed;\r\n    director.setSpeed(mode === \"full\" ? 0.5 : mode === \"gentle\" ? 0.8 : 2);\r\n    try {\r\n      for (const scene of trailer) {\r\n        if (trailerCancelled.current || !root.current) break;\r\n        setTrailerCard(scene.replaceAll(\"-\", \" \"));\r\n        const operation = scene === \"player-access\" ? async () => ({ safe: true }) : undefined;\r\n        await director.play(scene, { root: root.current, operation, queue: false });\r\n      }\r\n      setTrailerCard(\"GSAP Â· StPageFlip Â· Motion Â· Rive Â· Lottie\");\r\n    } catch (cause) {\r\n      if (!trailerCancelled.current)\r\n        setErrors((items) => [...items, cause instanceof Error ? cause.message : \"Trailer failed\"]);\r\n    } finally {\r\n      director.setSpeed(originalSpeed);\r\n      setTrailerPlaying(false);\r\n    }\r\n  }, [director, mode, snapshot.speed, trailerPlaying]);\r\n\r\n  useEffect(() => {\r\n    const keydown = (event: KeyboardEvent) => {\r\n      const target = event.target as HTMLElement;\r\n      if ([\"INPUT\", \"SELECT\", \"TEXTAREA\"].includes(target.tagName)) return;\r\n      if (event.code === \"Space\") {\r\n        event.preventDefault();\r\n        if (snapshot.isPaused) director.resume();\r\n        else if (snapshot.isPlaying) director.pause();\r\n        else void play();\r\n      }\r\n      if (event.key.toLowerCase() === \"r\") {\r\n        director.seek(0);\r\n        if (!snapshot.isPlaying) void play();\r\n      }\r\n      if (event.key.toLowerCase() === \"s\") director.skip();\r\n      if (event.key === \"ArrowLeft\") director.seek(Math.max(0, snapshot.progress - 0.05));\r\n      if (event.key === \"ArrowRight\") director.seek(Math.min(1, snapshot.progress + 0.05));\r\n      if (event.key === \"Escape\" && document.fullscreenElement) void document.exitFullscreen();\r\n    };\r\n    window.addEventListener(\"keydown\", keydown);\r\n    return () => window.removeEventListener(\"keydown\", keydown);\r\n  }, [director, play, snapshot.isPaused, snapshot.isPlaying, snapshot.progress]);\r\n\r\n  function reset() {\r\n    director.cancel(\"showcase-reset\");\r\n    director.setSpeed(1);\r\n    trailerCancelled.current = true;\r\n    setTrailerPlaying(false);\r\n    setErrors([]);\r\n    setAssetStatus({});\r\n    setTrailerCard(\"Animation architecture ready\");\r\n    setRuntimeEpoch((value) => value + 1);\r\n    resetAnimationMetrics();\r\n    book.current?.turnTo(0);\r\n    lottie.current?.stop();\r\n", "type": "input_text"}]

const r = await tools.mcp__node_repl__js({title:"Exercise every showcase demo",timeout_ms:120000,code:`var reducedModeButton = tab.playwright.getByRole("button",{name:"reduced",exact:true});
var reducedModeCount = await reducedModeButton.count();
var catalogSceneLabel = tab.playwright.locator(".showcase-catalog label").filter({hasText:"Scene"});
var catalogSceneLabelCount = await catalogSceneLabel.count();
var catalogSceneSelect = catalogSceneLabel.locator("select",{});
var catalogSceneSelectCount = await catalogSceneSelect.count();
var playSceneButton = tab.playwright.locator(".play-scene");
var playSceneButtonCount = await playSceneButton.count();
var speedLabel = tab.playwright.locator(".animation-controls label").filter({hasText:"Speed"});
var speedLabelCount = await speedLabel.count();
var speedSelect = speedLabel.locator("select",{});
var speedSelectCount = await speedSelect.count();
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify({reducedModeCount,catalogSceneLabelCount,catalogSceneSelectCount,playSceneButtonCount,speedLabelCount,speedSelectCount})+"\\n");
if (reducedModeCount===1) await reducedModeButton.click({});
if (speedSelectCount===1) await speedSelect.selectOption("2");
var demoRunResults = [];
for (const option of sceneOptions) {
  if (catalogSceneSelectCount!==1 || playSceneButtonCount!==1) break;
  await catalogSceneSelect.selectOption(option.value);
  await playSceneButton.click({});
  var idle=false;
  var observedScene="";
  for (var attempt=0;attempt<30;attempt++) {
    await tab.playwright.waitForTimeout(100);
    var readout = await tab.playwright.evaluate(()=>({scene:document.querySelector(".animation-readout span")?.textContent||"",label:document.querySelector(".animation-readout strong")?.textContent||"",errors:Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(".error-log li")).map(e=>e.textContent)}));
    observedScene=readout.scene;
    if (readout.scene==="No scene") { idle=true; break; }
  }
  demoRunResults.push({id:option.value,label:option.label,idle,observedScene});
}
var finalShowcaseState = await tab.playwright.evaluate(()=>({
  readout:document.querySelector(".animation-readout")?.textContent,
  errors:Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(".error-log li")).map(e=>e.textContent),
  metrics:Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(".metric-card, .metrics-grid div")).map(e=>e.textContent).filter(Boolean).slice(0,30),
  statuses:Array.from(document.querySelectorAll("[data-lottie-status],[data-pageflip-status],[data-rive-status],[data-fallback-active]")).map(e=>({className:e.className,status:e.getAttribute("data-lottie-status")||e.getAttribute("data-pageflip-status")||e.getAttribute("data-rive-status")||"fallback"})).slice(0,30)
}));
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify({demoRunResults,finalShowcaseState},null,2));`});
for (const c of (r?.content ?? [])) if (c.type === "text") text(c.text);

}))`});

Earlier Browser use does not make later semantic work browser-first. Before each semantic operation on a linked resource, you MUST query available and deferred tools for an applicable connector, API, or CLI. Reading this skill or scanning visible tools does not count. Do not initialize Browser for that operation until the query is complete. Use the non-browser tool when available. If it handles the current operation, continue the larger workflow without Browser for that operation. Use Browser when no such tool exists, the tool cannot access the resource or lacks a required capability, or UI work remains; use available browser context before asking the user to repeat it.\n\nUse this skill for browser automation tasks such as inspecting pages, navigating, testing local apps, clicking, typing, taking screenshots, and reading visible page state.\r\n\r\nIf this plugin is listed as available in the session, treat that as mandatory reading before browser work. Open and follow this skill before saying that Browser is unavailable and before falling back to standalone Playwright or Computer Use.\r\n\r\nDo not skip this skill just because Computer Use MCP tool calls are directly visible or appear easier to invoke. The presence of Computer Use tools is not evidence that Computer Use is the preferred browser surface.\n\n## Setup Documentation\nUse `await agent.documentation.get(\"<name>\")` when one of these setup topics applies:\n- `bootstrap-troubleshooting`: read when browser setup succeeds but discovery or selection fails\n- `chrome-troubleshooting`: read when Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication fails\n\n## Bootstrap\r\nThese setup details are internal. User-facing progress updates should be less technical in nature. Never mention `Node REPL`, `node_repl`, `REPL`, JavaScript sessions, module exports, reading documentation, or loading instructions unless a user is asking for that exact information. If setup or recovery is needed, describe it naturally as connecting to the browser or retrying the browser connection.\r\n\r\nThe `browser-client` module is the core entry point for browser use, and is available under `scripts/browser-client.mjs` in this plugin's root directory. ALWAYS import it using an absolute path. IMPORTANT: If this path cannot be found, stop and report that this plugin is missing `scripts/browser-client.mjs`. NEVER use the built in `browser-client` library.\r\n\r\nRun browser setup code through the Node REPL `js` tool. In this environment the callable tool id typically appears as `mcp__node_repl__js`. If it is not already available, use tool discovery for `node_repl js` without setting a result limit. You need the `js` execution tool: `js_reset` only clears state, and `js_add_node_module_dir` only changes package resolution. Do not call either helper while trying to expose `js`. If `js` is still not available, search again for `node_repl js` with `limit: 10`.\r\n\r\nInitialize the runtime once per fresh Node session. If `agent.browsers` already exists, reuse it; do not import or initialize another browser runtime.\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {\r\n  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import(\"<plugin root>/scripts/browser-client.mjs\");\r\n  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnce a browser connection is established, reuse its existing browser binding across later turns and do not reread this skill. Once you have read a browser's complete documentation, do not read it again unless you select a different browser.\r\n\r\nA tab binding is separate from its browser binding. If a later turn reports that a tab is missing, stale, closed, or not part of the current browser session, discard that tab binding and obtain or create a fresh tab from the existing browser binding. An empty `browser.tabs.list()` or `browser.user.openTabs()` result is normal after tab cleanup and does not invalidate the browser binding. Never call `agent.browsers.get*` to recover a tab; only an explicit browser-disconnected error invalidates the binding.\r\n\r\n## Browser selection\r\n\r\nThe scenarios below are for the initial browser selection only. Before calling any `agent.browsers.get*` method, reuse an existing `globalThis.browser`, `globalThis.iab`, or `globalThis.chrome` binding that already serves the task. A new user turn does not invalidate a browser binding or require another selection or documentation call.\r\n\r\nSelect the initial browser with exactly one of these scenarios, in the order\r\nshown. An explicit request for the in-app browser or Chrome always wins over URL\r\nselection. Never call `getForUrl()` when the user names a browser.\r\n\r\nApp-provided in-app-browser context is ambient UI state, not a user instruction to select or switch browsers. Only the text of the user's request can explicitly choose a browser.\r\n\r\nDo not inspect browser cookies, local storage, profiles, passwords, or session stores. Browser discovery must remain read-only.\r\n\r\nWhen authentication blocks requested browser navigation, do not replace it with web search, a search engine, another site, or another source merely to bypass sign-in.\r\n\r\n### The user explicitly requests a browser\r\n\r\nA plugin mention in the user's request explicitly names its browser.\r\n`[@Browser](plugin://browser@openai-bundled)` names the in-app browser.\r\n`[@Chrome](plugin://chrome@openai-bundled)`,\r\n`[@chrome-internal](plugin://chrome-internal@openai-bundled)`, and\r\n`[@chrome-dev](plugin://chrome-dev@openai-bundled)` name Chrome. Follow the\r\ncorresponding explicit-browser scenario below.\r\n\r\nThe in-app browser is available only when the Browser skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests the in-app browser and that skill is available, use a distinct persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.iab == null) {\r\n  globalThis.iab = await agent.browsers.get(\"iab\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await iab.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests the in-app browser but the Browser skill is not available, report that the in-app browser is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nChrome is available only when the Chrome skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests Chrome and that skill is available, use a separate persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.chrome == null) {\r\n  globalThis.chrome = await agent.browsers.get(\"extension\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await chrome.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests Chrome but the Chrome skill is not available, report that Chrome is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nAn explicit browser choice remains in force for the task. If authentication blocks the task in an explicitly selected browser, your next response must explicitly ask the user to sign in in that browser and tell you when it is ready, unless that browser's documentation provides a supported authentication flow to try first. Merely reporting that sign-in is required is not sufficient. Do not switch to another browser unless the user asks or approves the switch.\r\n\r\n### The task requires browser interaction, the user does not specify a browser, and the task has a target URL\r\n\r\nWhen the user supplies a URL or the intended URL can be reasonably inferred from the request, replace the example below with that URL and let browser-client choose the browser best suited to it:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getForUrl(\"https://example.com/\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n### The user specifies neither a browser nor a target URL\r\n\r\nUse the runtime default, which prefers the in-app browser when it is available and otherwise uses Chrome. Do not list browsers first:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getDefault();\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n## After setup\r\n\r\nIf setup succeeds but browser discovery or selection fails, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"bootstrap-troubleshooting\")` before resetting the JavaScript session or trying another browser-control mechanism.\r\n\r\nIf the failure is specific to Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"chrome-troubleshooting\")` before retrying or taking another recovery action.\r\n\r\nWhen the user did not explicitly choose a browser, a browser selected by the runtime is not a user constraint. Do not switch browsers based only on an assumption about authentication. If navigation shows that the selected browser lacks the required authentication, select another available browser before asking the user to sign in. You may select it without resetting the Node session. Preserve existing `iab`, `chrome`, and `browser` bindings when they are still useful. Existing tabs remain bound to the browser that created them. After selecting a different browser, obtain a tab from that browser before continuing and read its complete documentation.\r\n\r\nThe ability to interact directly with browsers is exposed through the `browser-client` runtime via the `agent.browsers.*` API. Before trying to interact with a selected browser for the first time, you MUST emit and read the complete documentation returned by its `documentation()` call in one go. For the initial documentation read, run the exact direct `nodeRepl.write(await <browser>.documentation());` call shown in the applicable scenario above. Do not assign the documentation to a variable, inspect its length, slice it, truncate it, summarize it, or emit only an excerpt. Do not proactively split the documentation into pages or chunks. const r = await tools.mcp__node_repl__js({title:"Exercise every showcase demo",timeout_ms:60000,code:`var showcaseValues2 = ["arrival","reentry","access-listening","access-success","access-failure","gm-success","gm-failure","journal-open","manual-flip","programmatic-flip","heading","prose","seal","release","map","route","marker","ship","silhouette","artifact","inspection","connection","quest","quest-complete","log","finale","finale-requirement","pause","resume","undo","motion-nav","rive-inputs","lottie-controls","pageflip-modes","reduced","fallbacks"];
var speedSelect2 = auditTab2.playwright.getByRole("combobox",{name:"Speed"});
await speedSelect2.selectOption({value:"2"});
var playSelected2 = auditTab2.playwright.getByRole("button",{name:"Play selected scene"});
var compactDemoResults2 = [];
for (var demoValue2 of showcaseValues2) {
  await sceneSelect2.selectOption({value:demoValue2});
  await playSelected2.click();
  await auditTab2.playwright.waitForTimeout({timeout:500});
  var demoState2 = JSON.parse(await auditTab2.playwright.evaluate((v) => {
    const text = document.body.innerText;
    const errorHeading = [...document.querySelectorAll("h2")].find(x=>x.textContent==="Error log");
    const errorText = errorHeading?.nextElementSibling?.textContent || "";
    const assetHeading = [...document.querySelectorAll("h2")].find(x=>x.textContent==="Asset status");
    return JSON.stringify({v,errorText,hasAlert:text.includes("Unhandled Runtime Error"),assetText:assetHeading?.parentElement?.innerText?.slice(-500)||""});
  }, demoValue2));
  compactDemoResults2.push(demoState2);
}
nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify({
  count: compactDemoResults2.length,
  withErrors: compactDemoResults2.filter(x=>x.hasAlert || !/No runtime errors/i.test(x.errorText)).map(x=>({v:x.v,error:x.errorText})),
  last: compactDemoResults2.at(-1)
}))`});
for (const c of (r.content||[])) if (c.type==="text") text(c.text);

const r = await tools.mcp__node_repl__js({title:"Measure duplicated scene targets",code:`nodeRepl.write(await auditTab2.playwright.evaluate(() => {
  const nodes=[...document.querySelectorAll("[data-scene-part]")];
  const by={};
  for(const n of nodes){
    const k=n.getAttribute("data-scene-part")||"";
    const r=n.getBoundingClientRect(); const s=getComputedStyle(n);
    const rec=by[k] ||= {total:0,visible:0,source:0,host:0,owned:0};
    rec.total++; if(s.display!=="none" && s.visibility!=="hidden" && r.width>0 && r.height>0) rec.visible++;
    if(n.closest(".page-flip-source")) rec.source++;
    if(n.closest(".page-flip-host")) rec.host++;
    if(n.hasAttribute("data-gsap-owned")) rec.owned++;
  }

}))`});

const r = await tools.mcp__node_repl__js({title:"Measure full-motion PageFlip targets",code:`nodeRepl.write(await auditTab2.playwright.evaluate(() => {
  const nodes=[...document.querySelectorAll("[data-scene-part]")];
  const by={};
  for(const n of nodes){
    const k=n.getAttribute("data-scene-part")||"";
    const r=n.getBoundingClientRect(); const s=getComputedStyle(n);
    const rec=by[k] ||= {total:0,visible:0,hiddenAncestor:0,owned:0,classes:[]};
    rec.total++; if(s.display!=="none" && s.visibility!=="hidden" && r.width>0 && r.height>0) rec.visible++;
    if(n.closest('[style*="display: none"],[hidden],.page-flip-source')) rec.hiddenAncestor++;
    if(n.hasAttribute("data-gsap-owned")) rec.owned++;
    rec.classes.push(n.parentElement?.className||"");
  }
  return JSON.stringify({total:nodes.length,duplicates:Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(by).filter(([,v])=>v.total>1)),pageClasses:[...document.querySelectorAll("[class]")].map(n=>n.className).filter(x=>typeof x==="string"&&/page-flip|stf__/i.test(x)).slice(0,50)});
}))`});
for (const c of (r.content||[])) if (c.type==="text") text(c.text);

The final matrix must be much more detailed.\r\n\r\n## Category A: Animation does not run at all\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* scene is registered but never called,\r\n* component exists but is never before animation begins,\r\n* an animation is development-only,\r\n* production renders only a fallback,\r\n* event type has no scene mapping.\r\n\r\n## Category B: Animation technically runs but is invisible\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* GSAP animates an empty target array,\r\n* the target is in a hidden source tree,\r\n* the animation runs in an unmounted section,\r\n* the target is behind another layer,\r\n* opacity or display prevents visibility,\r\n* a cloned PageFlip source receives the animation instead of the visible page,\r\n* broad selectors animate offscreen elements,\r\n* the scene completes semantically while no visible object moved.\r\n\r\n## Category C: Animation runs poorly\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* abrupt,\r\n* too fast,\r\n* too slow,\r\n* visually weak,\r\n* generic fade,\r\n* disconnected from the physical object,\r\n* poor easing,\r\n* no anticipation,\r\n* no settle,\r\n* no secondary motion,\r\n* timing does not match sound,\r\n* animation begins before the user can see it,\r\n* animation restarts unexpectedly,\r\n* animation ends with a snap,\r\n* transition does not match the visual design,\r\n* animation looks different depending on current section.\r\n\r\n## Category D: Animation only runs once\r\n\r\nDetermine whether each one-shot is:\r\n\r\n* intentionally ceremonial,\r\n* unintentionally blocked,\r\n* session-only,\r\n* tab-only,\r\n* device-only,\r\n* account-only,\r\n* campaign-only,\r\n* event-only,\r\n* replayable,\r\n* not replayable,\r\n* replayable only before refresh,\r\n* incorrectly replayed automatically.\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* `sessionStorage`,\r\n* `localStorage`,\r\n* component-local refs,\r\n* event acknowledgement,\r\n* sequence tracking,\r\n* route remount behavior,\r\n* React key behavior,\r\n* CSS animation iteration counts,\r\n* viewport `once` options,\r\n* persisted â€œseenâ€ flags.\r\n\r\n## Category E: Animation does not replay correctly\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* replay button disappears after refresh,\r\n* replay works only from the Journal,\r\n* replay mutates server state,\r\n* replay uses stale event data,\r\n* replay does not restore previous section,\r\n* replay leaves elements in a transformed state,\r\n* replay works once but fails a second time,\r\n* replay conflicts with a live event.\r\n\r\n## Category F: Wrong library owns the behavior\r\n\r\nFor every questionable animation, identify:\r\n\r\n* current owner,\r\n* correct owner,\r\n* reason,\r\n* migration recommendation,\r\n* wrappers or bridge required.\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* Motion and GSAP writing transform on the same node,\r\n* GSAP page-flip scenes duplicating StPageFlip,\r\n* CSS controlling narrative order,\r\n* Lottie autoplaying independently of a GSAP ceremony,\r\n* static SVG used where a stateful Rive object is promised.\r\n\r\n## Category G: Runtime ownership conflict\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* GSAP versus Motion,\r\n* GSAP versus CSS transitions,\r\n* Motion layout versus StPageFlip,\r\n* Motion layoutId versus GSAP FLIP,\r\n* hidden PageFlip sources versus visible clones,\r\n* Rive container transforms versus external transforms,\r\n* Lottie containers moved by the wrong owner.\r\n\r\nIdentify every element with overlapping property writers.\r\n\r\n## Category H: Missing trigger\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* scene exists but has no event,\r\n* page turn occurs but no lifecycle scene is emitted,\r\n* inspection scene exists but opening the inspection never calls it,\r\n* ship-course exists but current position never triggers it,\r\n* marker-stamp exists but all map events use map-reveal,\r\n* prose-ink exists but annotations appear instantly.\r\n\r\n## Category I: Missing cleanup or lifecycle handling\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* component unmount,\r\n* route changes,\r\n* tab visibility,\r\n* element visibility,\r\n* interrupted animations,\r\n* skipped animations,\r\n* reversed animations,\r\n* failed requests,\r\n* runtime destruction,\r\n* duplicated event listeners,\r\n* stale refs,\r\n* active timeouts,\r\n* unfinished Web Animations API promises,\r\n* Lottie and Rive instance counts,\r\n* PageFlip instance leaks,\r\n* GSAP context reversion.\r\n\r\n## Category J: Reduced-motion defect\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* browser `prefers-reduced-motion`,\r\n* product full/gentle/reduced setting,\r\n* MotionConfig,\r\n* CSS media queries,\r\n* GSAP duration scaling,\r\n* PageFlip fallback,\r\n* Lottie representative frames,\r\n* Rive stable poses,\r\n* sound behavior,\r\n* keyboard and focus behavior.\r\n\r\nReduced mode must preserve narrative order and readable state without unnecessary spatial travel.\r\n\r\n## Category K: Performance problem\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* main-thread long tasks,\r\n* duplicate runtime mounting,\r\n* mode-change recreation,\r\n* unnecessary JSON reloads,\r\n* repeated layout reads,\r\n* FLIP layout thrashing,\r\n* excessive SVG nodes,\r\n* broad selectors,\r\n* hidden elements being animated,\r\n* large blur/filter costs,\r\n* mobile GPU pressure,\r\n* memory after repeated replay and navigation.\r\n\r\n## Category L: Animation is missing and should be added\r\n\r\nCreate a very large list of missing animation opportunities for every screen.\r\n\r\nEach recommendation must identify:\r\n\r\n* screen,\r\n* user action or event,\r\n* proposed animation,\r\n* correct library,\r\n* trigger,\r\n* replay policy,\r\n* reduced-motion alternative,\r\n* priority,\r\n* why it improves the experience.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 5. Baseline Findings That Must Be Verified\r\n\r\nThe following issues were identified during an external source audit. Treat these as high-priority hypotheses that must be independently verified against the exact commit.\r\n\r\nDo not blindly repeat them. Confirm, refine, disprove or expand them with code and runtime evidence.\r\n\r\n## 5.1 Production Rive assets may all be fallbacks\r\n\r\nVerify whether the following production contracts have actual `.riv` paths:\r\n\r\n* invitation seal,\r\n* journal clasp,\r\n* voyage compass,\r\n* finale mechanism.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether real product screens are receiving only static SVG fallback content.\r\n\r\nDocument the exact screens affected and whether the UI misleadingly appears to use Rive when it does not.\r\n\r\n## 5.2 New platform sign-in pages may bypass cinematic authentication\r\n\r\nCompare:\r\n\r\n* modern Player sign-in,\r\n* modern Captain/Creator sign-in,\r\n* legacy Player AccessGate,\r\n* legacy Quartermaster login.\r\n\r\nDetermine which flows call AnimationDirector and which perform direct fetch-and-route behavior.\r\n\r\n## 5.3 Invitation Ceremony may currently be a static form\r\n\r\nVerify whether invitation acceptance, decline, PIN validation, invalid state, expiration and route transitions use any of:\r\n\r\n* GSAP,\r\n* Motion,\r\n* Rive,\r\n* Lottie,\r\n* CSS state sequences.\r\n\r\n## 5.4 Registered scenes may exist only in the showcase\r\n\r\nInvestigate these scenes:\r\n\r\n* `journal-open`\r\n* `manual-page-flip`\r\n* `programmatic-page-flip`\r\n* `chapter-heading`\r\n* `prose-ink`\r\n* `seal-break`\r\n* `marker-stamp`\r\n* `ship-course`\r\n* `artifact-inspection`\r\n\r\nFor each, document all real product call sites.\r\n\r\nIf there are none, mark the scene as dead, showcase-only, legacy-only or future-contract-only.\r\n\r\n## 5.5 Chapter release may be invisible outside the Journal\r\n\r\nVerify what happens when `CHAPTER_RELEASED` arrives while the Player is viewing:\r\n\r\n* Chart,\r\n* Treasures,\r\n* Quests,\r\n* Log,\r\n* Finale.\r\n\r\nThe chapter ceremony content appears to be mounted only inside JournalWorkspace.\r\n\r\nTest whether the scene:\r\n\r\n* animates nothing,\r\n* animates unrelated background targets,\r\n* remains invisible,\r\n* moves the user to Journal,\r\n* or uses a global overlay.\r\n\r\n## 5.6 Ceremony replay may fail outside Journal\r\n\r\nTest Replay Ceremony while each Player section is active.\r\n\r\nAlso test replay:\r\n\r\n* immediately,\r\n* twice in succession,\r\n* after route navigation,\r\n* after browser refresh,\r\n* after motion-mode change,\r\n* during another live event.\r\n\r\n## 5.7 Replay state may disappear after refresh\r\n\r\nVerify whether `lastRelease` or equivalent replay information is reconstructed from a safe server snapshot or exists only in component memory.\r\n\r\n## 5.8 Generic scene-part names may animate duplicates\r\n\r\nSearch for duplicate use of:\r\n\r\n* `route-path`\r\n* `map-fog`\r\n* `map-marker-new`\r\n* `artifact-reveal`\r\n* `artifact-light`\r\n* `artifact-slot-target`\r\n* `artifact-connection-path`\r\n* `quest-note-new`\r\n* `quest-stamp`\r\n* `red-thread`\r\n* `log-entry-new`\r\n* `log-symbol-new`\r\n* `finale-ring-outer`\r\n* `finale-ring-inner`\r\n* `finale-light-path`\r\n* `workspace-light`\r\n* `lantern`\r\n* `peripheral`\r\n\r\nDetermine whether a single scene animates:\r\n\r\n* a permanent screen target,\r\n* a temporary event overlay,\r\n* hidden source content,\r\n* and visible clones simultaneously.\r\n\r\n## 5.9 PageFlip source nodes may receive GSAP animation\r\n\r\nInspect the hidden React source used by `PageFlipBook` and the cloned visible StPageFlip DOM.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether animation attributes survive into both trees and whether GSAP can animate the hidden source instead of the visible page.\r\n\r\n## 5.10 Ownership may be warning-only\r\n\r\nVerify whether a rejected animation ownership claim actually prevents the animation from running.\r\n\r\nAudit whether Motion elements register ownership in the same runtime registry or merely include a data attribute.\r\n\r\n## 5.11 Confirm Motion and GSAP transform conflicts\r\n\r\nSpecifically inspect:\r\n\r\n* Voyage Chart marker buttons,\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log entries,\r\n* artifact inspection shared-layout object,\r\n* companion navigation,\r\n* Quartermaster action buttons,\r\n* any element with both Motion layout/layoutId and `data-gsap-owned`.\r\n\r\n## 5.12 PageFlip motion mode may not update correctly\r\n\r\nVerify whether changing from full to gentle mode updates `flippingTime` on an existing PageFlip instance.\r\n\r\nInspect effect dependencies and runtime recreation.\r\n\r\n## 5.13 Lottie may reload on every mode change\r\n\r\nVerify whether changing full/gentle/reduced:\r\n\r\n* destroys the runtime,\r\n* reloads JSON,\r\n* restarts ambient loops,\r\n* replays non-looping ink effects,\r\n* creates visible blank frames.\r\n\r\n## 5.14 CSS phase waiting may hang\r\n\r\nInspect `waitForJournalPhase()`.\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* abort during a phase,\r\n* infinite animation under an actor,\r\n* browser animation promise that never resolves,\r\n* missing actor,\r\n* missing transition,\r\n* reduced mode,\r\n* unmount during wait.\r\n\r\n## 5.15 Empty target arrays may silently pass\r\n\r\nDetermine which GSAP scenes can progress through all labels with no required visible targets.\r\n\r\nAdd evidence for scenes that â€œcompleteâ€ without moving anything.\r\n\r\n## 5.16 GSAP cleanup may cause final-state snapback\r\n\r\nTest access/login success and route-changing sequences.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether GSAP context reversion restores the closed seal, door or lock before navigation completes.\r\n\r\n## 5.17 Reduced-motion behavior may have multiple competing authorities\r\n\r\nInspect:\r\n\r\n* custom motion mode,\r\n* browser preference,\r\n* MotionConfig,\r\n* CSS media query,\r\n* PageFlip reduced fallback,\r\n* Lottie mode behavior,\r\n* Rive autoplay.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether any Motion component can still animate spatially under browser reduced motion because it forgot to consume the custom mode.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 6. Required Investigation Method\r\n\r\n## 6.1 Static source inventory\r\n\r\nSearch the entire source tree for:\r\n\r\n```text\r\ngsap\r\nuseGSAP\r\ndirector.play\r\nmotion.\r\n<motion\r\nAnimatePresence\r\nlayoutId\r\nlayout\r\nwhileHover\r\nwhileTap\r\npage-flip\r\nPageFlipBook\r\nRiveStatefulObject\r\nuseRive\r\nLottieEffect\r\nlottie-web\r\n@keyframes\r\nanimation:\r\ntransition:\r\ngetAnimations\r\nsessionStorage\r\nlocalStorage\r\nprefers-reduced-motion\r\nrequestAnimationFrame\r\nsetTimeout\r\nsetInterval\r\ndata-scene-part\r\ndata-gsap-owned\r\ndata-animation-owner\r\ndata-opening-actor\r\ndata-journal-phase\r\ndata-cinematic\r\n```\r\n\r\nCreate a complete inventory of:\r\n\r\n* animation files,\r\n* animation call sites,\r\n* scene definitions,\r\n* scene consumers,\r\n* animation assets,\r\n* CSS keyframes,\r\n* transition-based state machines,\r\n* replay flags,\r\n* one-shot flags,\r\n* fallback paths,\r\n* test coverage.\r\n\r\n## 6.2 Scene reachability graph\r\n\r\nFor every registered scene, produce:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nScene name\r\nDefinition file\r\nReal production call sites\r\nDevelopment-only call sites\r\nRequired targets\r\nOptional targets\r\nCurrent root scope\r\nTriggering event/action\r\nReplay path\r\nReduced behavior\r\nKnown conflicts\r\nReachability status\r\n```\r\n\r\nReachability status must be one of:\r\n\r\n* Production active\r\n* Legacy active\r\n* Development showcase only\r\n* Registered but unreachable\r\n* Partially integrated\r\n* Fallback-only\r\n* Dead or redundant\r\n* Unknown pending runtime test\r\n\r\n## 6.3 Live browser testing\r\n\r\nWhere possible, run the real application on port 3000 and test with browser automation and manual inspection.\r\n\r\nDo not rely solely on unit tests.\r\n\r\nCapture evidence at meaningful states, not arbitrary delays.\r\n\r\nTest at minimum:\r\n\r\n* 2560 Ã— 1440\r\n* 1920 Ã— 1080\r\n* 1440 Ã— 900\r\n* 430 Ã— 932\r\n* 390 Ã— 844\r\n* 844 Ã— 390\r\n\r\nTest:\r\n\r\n* full motion,\r\n* gentle motion,\r\n* product reduced motion,\r\n* browser reduced motion,\r\n* sound enabled,\r\n* sound disabled,\r\n* cached assets,\r\n* uncached assets,\r\n* first visit,\r\n* return visit,\r\n* refresh,\r\n* back/forward navigation,\r\n* replay,\r\n* skip,\r\n* failed requests,\r\n* slow requests,\r\n* offline/reconnect,\r\n* route unmount during animation.\r\n\r\nUse screenshots, Playwright traces, console output and performance evidence where appropriate.\r\n\r\nStore temporary evidence under an ignored audit directory such as:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nartifacts/animation-audit/\r\n```\r\n\r\nDo not commit large generated traces or screenshots unless explicitly required by repository documentation policy.\r\n\r\n## 6.4 Visible-target verification\r\n\r\nFor each cinematic scene, record at runtime:\r\n\r\n* required target count,\r\n* optional target count,\r\n* visible target count,\r\n* duplicate target count,\r\n* target bounding boxes,\r\n* target opacity,\r\n* target display/visibility state,\r\n* current Player section,\r\n* scene instance ID,\r\n* animation owner,\r\n* actual properties animated.\r\n\r\nA scene must not be considered visually successful merely because its timeline completed.\r\n\r\n## 6.5 Replay testing\r\n\r\nFor every replayable scene:\r\n\r\n1. Play once.\r\n2. Replay immediately.\r\n3. Replay again.\r\n4. Navigate away and replay.\r\n5. Refresh and replay.\r\n6. Switch motion mode and replay.\r\n7. Skip midway and replay.\r\n8. Interrupt with another scene.\r\n9. Verify no server mutation occurred.\r\n10. Verify cleanup returned all runtime counts to expected values.\r\n\r\n## 6.6 One-shot analysis\r\n\r\nFor every animation that does not automatically repeat, determine exactly why.\r\n\r\nDo not write vague statements such as â€œruns once.â€\r\n\r\nWrite:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nRuns once per:\r\n- component mount\r\n- browser tab\r\n- sessionStorage key\r\n- device\r\n- campaign\r\n- acknowledged event\r\n- route visit\r\n- application session\r\n```\r\n\r\nAlso document whether that policy is correct.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 7. Required Per-Animation Matrix\r\n\r\nCreate a master matrix with one row per animation, effect, transition or missing opportunity.\r\n\r\nThe matrix must include at least these fields:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nID\r\nScreen\r\nRole\r\nComponent\r\nAnimation or proposed animation\r\nCurrent library\r\nCorrect library\r\nCurrent trigger\r\nExpected trigger\r\nCurrent replay policy\r\nRecommended replay policy\r\nRuns at all?\r\nVisible when run?\r\nRuns only once?\r\nWorks after refresh?\r\nWorks from every relevant section?\r\nFull mode result\r\nGentle mode result\r\nReduced mode result\r\nMobile result\r\nDesktop result\r\nRequired targets\r\nMissing targets\r\nDuplicate targets\r\nOwnership conflicts\r\nCleanup status\r\nFallback status\r\nPerformance concerns\r\nAccessibility concerns\r\nCurrent quality\r\nFailure severity\r\nRecommended change\r\nImplementation complexity\r\nImplementation priority\r\nTest coverage\r\nEvidence\r\nSource files\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe final matrix should be large. Do not impose an artificial item limit.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 8. Required Per-Library Audit\r\n\r\n## 8.1 GSAP audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every scene,\r\n* every call site,\r\n* every selector,\r\n* every required target,\r\n* every optional target,\r\n* every empty-target possibility,\r\n* every global-root problem,\r\n* every cleanup/reversion issue,\r\n* every skip issue,\r\n* every failure branch,\r\n* every success branch,\r\n* every idle loop,\r\n* every reversible scene,\r\n* every timeline that can visually snap back,\r\n* every scene that should be split or retired.\r\n\r\nRecommend:\r\n\r\n* dedicated scene hosts,\r\n* scene instance IDs,\r\n* required-target contracts,\r\n* playback policies,\r\n* final-state policies,\r\n* interruption priorities,\r\n* semantic audio labels,\r\n* runtime visual assertions.\r\n\r\n## 8.2 Motion audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* all presence transitions,\r\n* layout transitions,\r\n* shared-layout transitions,\r\n* buttons and press effects,\r\n* dialogs,\r\n* cards,\r\n* tabs,\r\n* filters,\r\n* list updates,\r\n* section navigation,\r\n* all missing Motion opportunities in the modern platform.\r\n\r\nFind every node where Motion and GSAP may both write:\r\n\r\n* transform,\r\n* opacity,\r\n* layout,\r\n* scale,\r\n* x/y,\r\n* clip path.\r\n\r\nRecommend wrapper separation.\r\n\r\n## 8.3 StPageFlip audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* initialization,\r\n* mode changes,\r\n* page updates,\r\n* orientation,\r\n* keyboard handling,\r\n* pending turn queue,\r\n* source cloning,\r\n* visible DOM,\r\n* hidden source DOM,\r\n* failure fallback,\r\n* reduced mode,\r\n* cleanup,\r\n* page preservation,\r\n* filter-induced recreation,\r\n* responsive behavior.\r\n\r\nDetermine whether manual/programmatic GSAP page-flip scenes should be removed.\r\n\r\n## 8.4 Rive audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every contract,\r\n* every real `.riv` asset,\r\n* every fallback,\r\n* every state machine,\r\n* every input,\r\n* every screen,\r\n* production versus development behavior.\r\n\r\nCreate a detailed specification for missing project-authored Rive assets:\r\n\r\n* Invitation Seal\r\n* Journal Clasp\r\n* Voyage Compass\r\n* Finale Mechanism\r\n\r\nInclude required states, inputs, transitions and reduced-motion poses.\r\n\r\n## 8.5 Lottie audit\r\n\r\nDocument:\r\n\r\n* every JSON asset,\r\n* every mount,\r\n* autoplay,\r\n* loop,\r\n* speed,\r\n* visibility pause,\r\n* document pause,\r\n* mode changes,\r\n* failure handling,\r\n* stalled-load handling,\r\n* timing relative to GSAP scenes.\r\n\r\nIdentify non-looping Lottie effects that begin before their intended semantic scene label.\r\n\r\n## 8.6 CSS audit\r\n\r\nInventory:\r\n\r\n* all keyframes,\r\n* all long transitions,\r\n* data-attribute state machines,\r\n* journal opening mechanics,\r\n* waiting-room breathing,\r\n* ambient effects,\r\n* focus/hover transitions,\r\n* reduced-motion overrides.\r\n\r\nSeparate:\r\n\r\n* material rendering,\r\n* ambient animation,\r\n* narrative orchestration.\r\n\r\nRecommend moving narrative ordering out of CSS without removing CSS material behavior.\r\n\r\n## 8.7 Web Animations API audit\r\n\r\nAudit:\r\n\r\n* use of `getAnimations`,\r\n* handling of `animation.finished`,\r\n* abort,\r\n* timeout,\r\n* infinite animations,\r\n* missing animation detection,\r\n* expected actor validation.\r\n\r\n## 8.8 Audio audit\r\n\r\nMap every sound cue to:\r\n\r\n* current trigger,\r\n* desired semantic label,\r\n* timing offset,\r\n* missing visual synchronization,\r\n* reduced/silent behavior.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 9. Required Missing-Animation Blueprint\r\n\r\nThe final report must include a massive list of animations that should be added.\r\n\r\nDo not merely list names. For each proposed addition include:\r\n\r\n```text\r\nScreen\r\nMoment\r\nUser benefit\r\nVisual concept\r\nCorrect library\r\nSupporting library if any\r\nTrigger\r\nDuration range\r\nReplay policy\r\nReduced-motion behavior\r\nSound cue\r\nFailure fallback\r\nPriority\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude additions for at least:\r\n\r\n* Landing and role selection\r\n* Player sign-in\r\n* Captain sign-in\r\n* Creator sign-in\r\n* Invitation ceremony\r\n* Player Library\r\n* Captain Library\r\n* New Voyage wizard\r\n* Waiting room\r\n* Journal opening\r\n* Journal pages\r\n* Chapter release\r\n* Chart\r\n* Treasure Altar\r\n* Artifact inspection\r\n* Side quests\r\n* Shipâ€™s Log\r\n* Finale\r\n* Quartermaster commands\r\n* Studio authoring\r\n* loading/error/success states\r\n* shell navigation\r\n* route transitions\r\n* theme/color-scheme transitions\r\n* offline/reconnect\r\n* notification and unseen-content states\r\n\r\nDo not reduce the list to ten or twenty items. The goal is a comprehensive experience blueprint.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 10. Required Deliverables\r\n\r\nCreate the following files inside `Development_Docs`:\r\n\r\n## 10.1 Master audit\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Full_Audit.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nThis must contain:\r\n\r\n1. Executive summary\r\n2. Audit baseline and methodology\r\n3. Current architecture\r\n4. Current library inventory\r\n5. Scene reachability report\r\n6. Animations that do not run\r\n7. Animations that run invisibly\r\n8. Animations that run poorly\r\n9. One-shot and replay analysis\r\n10. Ownership conflicts\r\n11. Wrong-library findings\r\n12. Lifecycle and cleanup findings\r\n13. Reduced-motion findings\r\n14. Performance findings\r\n15. Per-screen audit\r\n16. Per-library audit\r\n17. Massive missing-animation list\r\n18. Recommended architecture\r\n19. Severity-ranked findings\r\n20. Evidence references\r\n21. Limitations and items requiring further runtime confirmation\r\n\r\n## 10.2 Detailed animation matrix\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Audit_Matrix.csv\r\n```\r\n\r\nThe CSV must be genuinely useful for implementation planning and must contain the required fields specified earlier.\r\n\r\nDo not create a CSV containing vague one-line summaries.\r\n\r\n## 10.3 Implementation roadmap\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Implementation_Roadmap.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nOrganize implementation into no more than six major phases.\r\n\r\nA recommended phase structure is:\r\n\r\n1. **Animation truth and broken-trigger repair**\r\n2. **Ownership and scene-scoping architecture**\r\n3. **Player cinematic integration**\r\n4. **Platform, Library and Invitation motion**\r\n5. **Rive/Lottie/PageFlip completion**\r\n6. **Performance, accessibility and final polish**\r\n\r\nFor each phase include:\r\n\r\n* exact scope,\r\n* dependencies,\r\n* files likely affected,\r\n* libraries involved,\r\n* acceptance criteria,\r\n* tests,\r\n* risks,\r\n* work that can happen in parallel,\r\n* work that must not happen in parallel.\r\n\r\n## 10.4 Animation test plan\r\n\r\n```text\r\nDevelopment_Docs/Animation_System_Test_Plan.md\r\n```\r\n\r\nInclude:\r\n\r\n* unit tests,\r\n* component tests,\r\n* end-to-end tests,\r\n* visual checkpoint tests,\r\n* target-integrity tests,\r\n* replay tests,\r\n* one-shot tests,\r\n* every-event/every-section matrix,\r\n* mode matrix,\r\n* viewport matrix,\r\n* interruption tests,\r\n* performance tests,\r\n* memory-leak tests,\r\n* fallback tests.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 11. Severity and Priority Rules\r\n\r\nUse these severity levels:\r\n\r\n## P0 - Broken core experience\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* a major ceremony is invisible,\r\n* animation blocks interaction indefinitely,\r\n* route cannot complete,\r\n* reduced-motion accessibility failure,\r\n* production asset silently does not exist,\r\n* server event is acknowledged despite no visible required presentation when presentation is mandatory.\r\n\r\n## P1 - Major quality or consistency failure\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* wrong object animates,\r\n* duplicate objects animate,\r\n* replay is unavailable,\r\n* animation differs unpredictably by section,\r\n* severe library ownership collision,\r\n* visible snapback,\r\n* page-turn setting does not apply.\r\n\r\n## P2 - Noticeable polish deficiency\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* weak easing,\r\n* generic fade,\r\n* missing secondary motion,\r\n* missing form transition,\r\n* poor loading state,\r\n* abrupt filtering.\r\n\r\n## P3 - Enhancement\r\n\r\nExamples:\r\n\r\n* richer ambient motion,\r\n* optional parallax,\r\n* subtle hover refinement,\r\n* additional material response.\r\n\r\nPrioritize correctness and consistency before adding decorative layers.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 12. Required Final Recommendations\r\n\r\nThe final audit must answer all of the following:\r\n\r\n1. Which animations are completely broken?\r\n2. Which animations do not run in production?\r\n3. Which animations run only in the development showcase?\r\n4. Which animations run but target invisible elements?\r\n5. Which animations depend on the Playerâ€™s current section?\r\n6. Which animations run once?\r\n7. Which one-shot policies are correct?\r\n8. Which one-shot policies should change?\r\n9. Which animations cannot replay after refresh?\r\n10. Which scenes have no real trigger?\r\n11. Which scenes should be retired?\r\n12. Which scenes should be connected?\r\n13. Which behaviors use the wrong library?\r\n14. Which nodes have conflicting owners?\r\n15. Which Rive objects are currently static fallbacks?\r\n16. Which Lottie effects restart incorrectly?\r\n17. Which PageFlip behaviors fail after mode changes?\r\n18. Which CSS phase waits can hang or skip?\r\n19. Which animations are missing from modern platform screens?\r\n20. What should be added to make the entire experience feel unified and premium?\r\n21. What exact implementation sequence should follow?\r\n22. Which work can be done in parallel?\r\n23. What automated tests are required before declaring the animation system complete?\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 13. Hard Boundaries\r\n\r\nDo not:\r\n\r\n* broadly implement fixes during this audit,\r\n* rewrite the entire animation architecture,\r\n* replace all libraries with GSAP,\r\n* replace all libraries with Motion,\r\n* delete Rive because final assets are missing,\r\n* replace StPageFlip with a homegrown book animation,\r\n* change unrelated product functionality,\r\n* alter narrative or personal content,\r\n* expose private content,\r\n* interfere with concurrent animation-fix work,\r\n* stage the entire repository,\r\n* claim runtime confirmation without actually running the relevant flow,\r\n* claim an animation works merely because a timeline completed,\r\n* claim a fallback is a real Rive animation,\r\n* produce only a short summary.\r\n\r\nTemporary non-invasive instrumentation is acceptable if required for investigation, but it must either:\r\n\r\n* remain isolated and clearly documented, or\r\n* be reverted before task completion.\r\n\r\nThe final committed application source should remain unchanged unless there is an extremely small audit-support correction that is separately justified. Documentation files are the intended output.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 14. Quality Bar\r\n\r\nThe audit is complete only when:\r\n\r\n* every registered scene has a reachability status,\r\n* every real animation call site has been inventoried,\r\n* every production animation library has a status,\r\n* all known hypotheses have been verified or disproved,\r\n* one-shot and replay behavior is documented per animation,\r\n* broken and invisible animation paths are identified,\r\n* ownership conflicts are mapped,\r\n* the modern platform and legacy cinematic paths are compared,\r\n* every major screen has a missing-animation blueprint,\r\n* the audit matrix is extensive,\r\n* the implementation roadmap is actionable,\r\n* and the test plan would prevent the same regressions from returning.\r\n\r\nThe result should be detailed enough that future Codex implementation prompts can use the audit as the governing source of truth and implement one phase at a time without guessing.\r\n\r\n---\r\n\r\n# 15. Final Response Format\r\n\r\nAt completion, provide:\r\n\r\n1. Exact baseline audited.\r\n2. Git/worktree safety status.\r\n3. Count of animation items inventoried.\r\n4. Count by category:\r\n\r\n   * broken,\r\n   * invisible,\r\n   * poor quality,\r\n   * one-shot,\r\n   * replay-defective,\r\n   * wrong library,\r\n   * ownership conflict,\r\n   * missing animation,\r\n   * needs runtime verification.\r\n5. Top P0 findings.\r\n6. Top P1 findings.\r\n7. Deliverable file paths.\r\n8. Tests and runtime flows executed.\r\n9. Anything that could not be verified.\r\n10. Confirmation that concurrent source work was preserved.\r\n11. Mandatory chat and Development_Docs synchronization results according to repository instructions.\r\n\r\nDo not end with vague language such as â€œthere may be opportunities for improvement.â€\r\n\r\nGive exact findings, exact evidence, exact files and exact next implementation phase.\r\n\r\n", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": "---git---", "type": "input_text"}, {"text": It can have existing signed-in sessions. For semantic operations on linked resources, prefer a purpose-built connector, API, or CLI when available.\"\r\n---\r\n\r\n# Browser\r\n## Stop: choose the right surface before any browser action\r\n\r\nExplicit browser intent wins: if the user names the in-app browser or Chrome, or asks to open, show, or navigate to a page; inspect its visual or interactive state; or interact with its UI, continue with Browser and do not substitute a connector.\r\n\r\nOtherwise, treat a URL or open browser tab as context, not browser intent. Earlier Browser use does not make later semantic work browser-first. Before each semantic operation on a linked resource, you MUST query available and deferred tools for an applicable connector, API, or CLI. Reading this skill or scanning visible tools does not count. Do not initialize Browser for that operation until the query is complete. Use the non-browser tool when available. If it handles the current operation, continue the larger workflow without Browser for that operation. Use Browser when no such tool exists, the tool cannot access the resource or lacks a required capability, or UI work remains; use available browser context before asking the user to repeat it.\n\nUse this skill for browser automation tasks such as inspecting pages, navigating, testing local apps, clicking, typing, taking screenshots, and reading visible page state.\r\n\r\nIf this plugin is listed as available in the session, treat that as mandatory reading before browser work. Open and follow this skill before saying that Browser is unavailable and before falling back to standalone Playwright or Computer Use.\r\n\r\nDo not skip this skill just because Computer Use MCP tool calls are directly visible or appear easier to invoke. The presence of Computer Use tools is not evidence that Computer Use is the preferred browser surface.\n\n## Setup Documentation\nUse `await agent.documentation.get(\"<name>\")` when one of these setup topics applies:\n- `bootstrap-troubleshooting`: read when browser setup succeeds but discovery or selection fails\n- `chrome-troubleshooting`: read when Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication fails\n\n## Bootstrap\r\nThese setup details are internal. User-facing progress updates should be less technical in nature. Never mention `Node REPL`, `node_repl`, `REPL`, JavaScript sessions, module exports, reading documentation, or loading instructions unless a user is asking for that exact information. If setup or recovery is needed, describe it naturally as connecting to the browser or retrying the browser connection.\r\n\r\nThe `browser-client` module is the core entry point for browser use, and is available under `scripts/browser-client.mjs` in this plugin's root directory. ALWAYS import it using an absolute path. IMPORTANT: If this path cannot be found, stop and report that this plugin is missing `scripts/browser-client.mjs`. NEVER use the built in `browser-client` library.\r\n\r\nRun browser setup code through the Node REPL `js` tool. In this environment the callable tool id typically appears as `mcp__node_repl__js`. If it is not already available, use tool discovery for `node_repl js` without setting a result limit. You need the `js` execution tool: `js_reset` only clears state, and `js_add_node_module_dir` only changes package resolution. Do not call either helper while trying to expose `js`. If `js` is still not available, search again for `node_repl js` with `limit: 10`.\r\n\r\nInitialize the runtime once per fresh Node session. If `agent.browsers` already exists, reuse it; do not import or initialize another browser runtime.\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {\r\n  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import(\"<plugin root>/scripts/browser-client.mjs\");\r\n  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nOnce a browser connection is established, reuse its existing browser binding across later turns and do not reread this skill. Once you have read a browser's complete documentation, do not read it again unless you select a different browser.\r\n\r\nA tab binding is separate from its browser binding. If a later turn reports that a tab is missing, stale, closed, or not part of the current browser session, discard that tab binding and obtain or create a fresh tab from the existing browser binding. An empty `browser.tabs.list()` or `browser.user.openTabs()` result is normal after tab cleanup and does not invalidate the browser binding. Never call `agent.browsers.get*` to recover a tab; only an explicit browser-disconnected error invalidates the binding.\r\n\r\n## Browser selection\r\n\r\nThe scenarios below are for the initial browser selection only. Before calling any `agent.browsers.get*` method, reuse an existing `globalThis.browser`, `globalThis.iab`, or `globalThis.chrome` binding that already serves the task. A new user turn does not invalidate a browser binding or require another selection or documentation call.\r\n\r\nSelect the initial browser with exactly one of these scenarios, in the order\r\nshown. An explicit request for the in-app browser or Chrome always wins over URL\r\nselection. Never call `getForUrl()` when the user names a browser.\r\n\r\nApp-provided in-app-browser context is ambient UI state, not a user instruction to select or switch browsers. Only the text of the user's request can explicitly choose a browser.\r\n\r\nDo not inspect browser cookies, local storage, profiles, passwords, or session stores. Browser discovery must remain read-only.\r\n\r\nWhen authentication blocks requested browser navigation, do not replace it with web search, a search engine, another site, or another source merely to bypass sign-in.\r\n\r\n### The user explicitly requests a browser\r\n\r\nA plugin mention in the user's request explicitly names its browser.\r\n`[@Browser](plugin://browser@openai-bundled)` names the in-app browser.\r\n`[@Chrome](plugin://chrome@openai-bundled)`,\r\n`[@chrome-internal](plugin://chrome-internal@openai-bundled)`, and\r\n`[@chrome-dev](plugin://chrome-dev@openai-bundled)` name Chrome. Follow the\r\ncorresponding explicit-browser scenario below.\r\n\r\nThe in-app browser is available only when the Browser skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests the in-app browser and that skill is available, use a distinct persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.iab == null) {\r\n  globalThis.iab = await agent.browsers.get(\"iab\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await iab.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests the in-app browser but the Browser skill is not available, report that the in-app browser is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nChrome is available only when the Chrome skill is listed for the session. If the user explicitly requests Chrome and that skill is available, use a separate persistent binding and immediately read its complete documentation:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.chrome == null) {\r\n  globalThis.chrome = await agent.browsers.get(\"extension\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await chrome.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\nIf the user explicitly requests Chrome but the Chrome skill is not available, report that Chrome is unavailable instead of substituting another browser.\r\n\r\nAn explicit browser choice remains in force for the task. If authentication blocks the task in an explicitly selected browser, your next response must explicitly ask the user to sign in in that browser and tell you when it is ready, unless that browser's documentation provides a supported authentication flow to try first. Merely reporting that sign-in is required is not sufficient. Do not switch to another browser unless the user asks or approves the switch.\r\n\r\n### The task requires browser interaction, the user does not specify a browser, and the task has a target URL\r\n\r\nWhen the user supplies a URL or the intended URL can be reasonably inferred from the request, replace the example below with that URL and let browser-client choose the browser best suited to it:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getForUrl(\"https://example.com/\");\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n### The user specifies neither a browser nor a target URL\r\n\r\nUse the runtime default, which prefers the in-app browser when it is available and otherwise uses Chrome. Do not list browsers first:\r\n\r\n```js\r\nif (globalThis.browser == null) {\r\n  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getDefault();\r\n  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());\r\n}\r\n```\r\n\r\n## After setup\r\n\r\nIf setup succeeds but browser discovery or selection fails, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"bootstrap-troubleshooting\")` before resetting the JavaScript session or trying another browser-control mechanism.\r\n\r\nIf the failure is specific to Chrome extension setup, installation, or communication, read `await agent.documentation.get(\"chrome-troubleshooting\")` before retrying or taking another recovery action.\r\n\r\nWhen the user did not explicitly choose a browser, a browser selected by the runtime is not a user constraint. Do not switch browsers based only on an assumption about authentication. If navigation shows that the selected browser lacks the required authentication, select another available browser before asking the user to sign in. You may select it without resetting the Node session. Preserve existing `iab`, `chrome`, and `browser` bindings when they are still useful. Existing tabs remain bound to the browser that created them. After selecting a different browser, obtain a tab from that browser before continuing and read its complete documentation.\r\n\r\nThe ability to interact directly with browsers is exposed through the `browser-client` runtime via the `agent.browsers.*` API. Before trying to interact with a selected browser for the first time, you MUST emit and read the complete documentation returned by its `documentation()` call in one go. For the initial documentation read, run the exact direct `nodeRepl.write(await <browser>.documentation());` call shown in the applicable scenario above. Do not assign the documentation to a variable, inspect its length, slice it, truncate it, summarize it, or emit only an excerpt. Do not proactively split the documentation into pages or chunks. Top-level bindings persist across `js` calls until `js_reset`; do not redeclare existing `const` or `let` names. Reuse existing bindings, use top-level `var` for reusable state that may be assigned again, or choose a fresh descriptive name. Use `js_add_node_module_dir` before `js` when a skill provides an extra package directory, and use dynamic imports like `await import(\\\"playwright\\\")` rather than filesystem paths under `./node_modules`.\\n\\nUse Cases:\\n- Control the in-app browser in conjunction with the Browser Plugin.\\n- Control the Chrome browser in conjunction with the Chrome Plugin. Prefer this method of controlling Chrome over alternatives (such as Computer Use) unless the user explicitly mentions an alternative.\\n\\nRun JavaScript in a persistent Node-backed kernel with top-level await. This is the JavaScript execution tool for the `node_repl` MCP server; use it whenever instructions say to use `node_repl`, the Node REPL MCP, or run Node REPL code. If `timeout_ms` is omitted, execution times out after 30000 ms (30 seconds); pass a larger `timeout_ms` for slow browser automation or other long-running operations. Use `nodeRepl.cwd`, `nodeRepl.homeDir`, and `nodeRepl.tmpDir` to inspect host paths. Use `nodeRepl.requestMeta` to inspect the current MCP request `_meta` object during a tool call. Use `nodeRepl.setResponseMeta(meta)` to attach top-level MCP result `_meta`; repeated calls shallow-merge object keys for the current tool call. Use `nodeRepl.write(value)` to add output without a newline. Strings are unchanged; other values use console-style formatting, including BigInt and circular objects. Prefer it over `console.log(...)` for final output; `console.log(...)` remains useful for debugging or multiple values. Use `await nodeRepl.emitImage(imageLike)` to return images; each call adds one image to the outer tool result, so call it multiple times to emit multiple images. Supported image inputs are a data URL, inferred PNG/JPEG/WebP bytes, or `{ bytes, mimeType }`. Saved references to `nodeRepl.write(...)` and `nodeRepl.emitImage(...)` stay reusable across calls, but async callbacks that fire after a call finishes still fail because no exec is active. Top-level bindings persist across calls until `js_reset`. If a call throws, prior bindings remain available and bindings that finished initializing before the throw often remain reusable. For reusable names that may be assigned again later, prefer top-level `var name = ...`; `var` can be redeclared across calls. If you hit `SyntaxError: Identifier 'x' has already been declared`, reuse the existing binding if possible, reassign it only if it was declared with `let` or `var`, or pick a new name instead of resetting immediately; a previous `const x` cannot be changed into `var x`. Use a short `{ ... }` block only for temporary scratch names, and do not wrap an entire call in block scope if you want those names reusable later. Use dynamic imports like `await import(\\\"playwright\\\")`, `await import(\\\"pkg\\\")`, or `await import(\\\"./file.js\\\")`; top-level static `import` is not supported. Import packages by package name after installing them into a directory added with `js_add_node_module_dir`, `NODE_REPL_NODE_MODULE_DIRS`, or the working directory. Do not import package entrypoints by filesystem path such as `./node_modules/playwright/index.mjs`. Imported local files must be ESM `.js` or `.mjs` files and run in the context chosen at their dynamic-import boundary, so they can also use `nodeRepl.*`, the captured `console`, and `import.meta` helpers. Bare imports from model code and local files resolve from the REPL-wide search roots (`NODE_REPL_NODE_MODULE_DIRS`, then directories later added with `js_add_node_module_dir`, then cwd); dependencies of trusted ESM packages use Node's package-relative lookup. Imported local files may statically import other local `.js` / `.mjs` files, available packages, and allowed Node builtins. `import.meta.resolve()` returns importable strings such as `file://...`, bare package names, and `node:...` specifiers. Local file modules reload between execs; trusted package entrypoints retain singleton identity. `node:` builtins are generally available via dynamic import, but model code cannot import `process` / `node:process` because the current Rust-server-to-Node-child transport runs over stdio and raw process streams can corrupt it. Trusted modules that import or reference `process` receive only a frozen metadata-only process shim with `arch`, `cwd()`, `env`, `pid`, and `platform`. Prefer `nodeRepl.write(...)` for text or formatted values and `nodeRepl.emitImage(...)` for images.\\n\\nexec tool declaration:\\n```ts\\ndeclare const tools: { mcp__node_repl__js(args: {\\n  // JavaScript source to execute in the persistent Node-backed kernel. The code runs with top-level await and can use the `nodeRepl` helpers. Examples: `nodeRepl.write(nodeRepl.cwd)`, `const { chromium } = await import(\\\"playwright\\\")`, or `await nodeRepl.emitImage(pngBuffer)`.\\n  code: string;\\n  // Optional execution timeout in milliseconds. Defaults to 30000 (30 seconds) when omitted.\\n  timeout_ms?: number;\\n  // Short user-facing description of what this code block is doing. ## Tool Call: exec
**Timestamp:** 2026-07-18T16:54:35.328Z

const r = await tools.mcp__node_repl__js({title:"Reconnect to audit browser",code:`if (globalThis.agent?.browsers == null) {
  const { setupBrowserRuntime } = await import("C:/Users/kkids/.codex/plugins/cache/openai-bundled/browser/26.715.31925/scripts/browser-client.mjs");
  await setupBrowserRuntime({ globals: globalThis });
}
if (globalThis.browser == null) {
  globalThis.browser = await agent.browsers.getForUrl("http://127.0.0.1:3100/");
  nodeRepl.write(await browser.documentation());
} else {
  nodeRepl.write(JSON.stringify({tabs: await browser.tabs.list()}));
}`});
for (const c of (r.content||[])) if (c.type==="text") text(c.text);

Submitting forms, sending messages, posting comments, uploading files, changing sharing/access, and entering sensitive data into third-party pages can transmit user data.\r\n- Before transmitting sensitive data such as contact details, addresses, passwords, OTPs, auth codes, API keys, payment data, financial or medical information, private identifiers, precise location, logs, memories, browsing/search history, or personal files, check whether the user's initial prompt clearly authorized sending those specific data to that specific destination. If so, proceed without asking again. Otherwise, confirm immediately before transmission.\r\n- Confirm at action-time before sending messages, submitting forms that create an external side effect, making purchases, changing permissions, uploading personal files, deleting nontrivial data, installing extensions/software, saving passwords, or saving payment methods.\r\n- Confirm before accepting browser permission prompts for camera, microphone, location, downloads, extension installation, or account/login access unless the user has already given narrow, task-specific approval.\r\n- For each CAPTCHA you see, ask the user whether they want you to solve it. Do not ask vague proceed-or-continue questions.\n\n\n# Browser Visibility Guidance\r\n\r\n- Keep browser work in the background by default.\r\n- Show the browser when the user's request is primarily to put a page in front of them or let them watch the interaction, such as opening a URL for them, showing the current tab, or keeping the browser visible while testing.\r\n- Do not show the browser when navigation is only a means to answer a question or verify behavior. Localhost targets and ordinary page navigation do not by themselves require visibility.\r\n- When the browser should be visible, call `await (await browser.capabilities.get(\"visibility\")).set(true)`.\n\n\n# User Tab Claiming\r\n\r\n- To take over an already-open in-app browser tab, call `browser.user.openTabs()`, choose the matching returned tab by its visible title and URL, then pass that exact object to `browser.user.claimTab(tab)`.\r\n- Claiming makes that existing tab part of the current Browser Use run and returns a normal controllable `Tab`. Reuse the returned tab for navigation, Playwright, screenshots, CUA, and content reads.\r\n- Do not pass `openTabs()` ids to `browser.tabs.get(...)`. `browser.tabs.get(...)` only resolves tabs that the current Browser Use run is already controlling.\r\n- Prefer claiming the existing in-app browser tab when the page you need is already open, instead of opening a duplicate tab to the same URL.\n\n\n# Tab Cleanup\r\n\r\n- Before ending a turn after in-app browser work with multiple tabs, call `browser.tabs.finalize({ keep })` when it is supported by the backend.\r\n- Treat `browser.tabs.finalize({ keep })` as the final browser action of the turn. Do not call browser tools after finalizing. If more browser work is needed, do it before finalizing, then finalize once with the final tab disposition.\r\n- Omit tabs by default. A tab is worth keeping only when the user needs that live page after the turn; otherwise leave it out of `keep`.\r\n- Omit research, search, source, intermediate, duplicate, blank, error, and login/navigation tabs after you have extracted what you need.\r\n- Keep a tab with `status: \"deliverable\"` when the tab itself is a user-facing output or requested open page. Deliverable tabs are left open after the current Browser Use run releases them.\r\n- Keep a tab with `status: \"handoff\"` only when the task is still in progress and the user or a later turn should continue from that live page.\n\n\n# All-Tabs Cleanup Guidance\r\n\r\n- If the user asks to close *all* visible browser tabs in the in-app browser, do not rely on `browser.user.openTabs()` alone. Close current-session tabs from `browser.tabs.list()`, and claim+close released or user tabs from `browser.user.openTabs()`.\n\n\n# Browser Control Interruption\r\n\r\n- If browser use is interrupted because the extension or user took control, do not quote the raw runtime error. Summarize it naturally for the user, for example: \"Browser use was stopped in the extension.\" Avoid internal terms like `turn_id`, runtime, retry, or plugin error text unless the user asks for details.\n\n\n# API Use\r\n\r\n## How to use the API\r\n\r\n* You are provided with various options for interacting with the browser (Playwright, vision), and you should use the most appropriate tool for the job.\r\n* Prefer Playwright where possible, but if it is not clear how to best use it, prefer vision.\r\n* Always make sure you understand what is on the screen before proceeding to your next action. Only ask clarifying questions if you really need to. After reloading, take a fresh DOM snapshot or screenshot before continuing.\r\n* Browsing history may prompt user approval. Each read crosses the browser boundary and becomes extremely expensive on large pages.\r\n- `locator.getAttribute(...)` is a single-element read, not a batch read. If the locator matches multiple elements, expect a strict-mode error rather than an array of attributes.\r\n- Use one broad observation to orient yourself: usually one fresh snapshot, or one screenshot if the visual structure is clearer than the DOM.\r\n- After that orientation step, narrow to the relevant section or a small number of strong candidates.\r\n- If the page is not getting narrower, do not scale up extraction across more elements. Change strategy instead.\r\n- Do not use `locator(...).allTextContents()`, `locator(\"body\").textContent()`, or `locator(\"body\").innerText()` as exploratory search tools across a page or large container.\r\n- Use broad text or attribute extraction only after you have already identified the exact container or element you need, and only when a smaller scoped check would not answer the question.\r\n- When you need many links, media URLs, or result titles, prefer a single `domSnapshot()` and parse the relevant lines, use the site's own search/filter UI, or navigate directly to a focused results page.
